📄 Complete Details & Notification

The SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 has been released by the Staff Selection Commission (SSC).

As per the latest updates, the final marks and scorecard were made available on February 17, 2026, at the official website ssc.gov.in. This follows the declaration of the SSC GD final result on January 15, 2026.

FOLLOW US

Candidates who appeared in the PST/PET and DV/DME/RME stages can now download their SSC GD final scorecard 2025 using their registration number and password. The scorecard displays normalized CBT marks and overall qualifying status after all recruitment stages. The download window is limited - typically a short period - so act quickly before the link deactivates.

Key Highlights:

  • Release Date: February 17, 2026
  • Last Date to Download: March 1, 2026 (till 6:00 PM)
  • Access: Online only via ssc.gov.in login
  • Purpose: View final marks, compare with cut-offs, and save for records/joining

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Overview

Particular Details
Exam Name Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA, Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles, and Sepoy in NCB Examination 2025
Conducting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Final Result Status Declared on January 15, 2026
Scorecard Status Released on February 17, 2026
Mode of Scorecard Online only
Login Credentials Registration number / Username and Password (plus captcha if required)
Official Website ssc.gov.in

Visit the Staff Selection Commission (SSC) official website – ssc.gov.in

Important Dates: SSC GD Final Result and Scorecard 2025

Event Date/Details
CBT Exam Dates February 4 to 25, 2025
PET/PST and Medical Stages Conducted post-CBT shortlisting
Final Result Declaration January 15, 2026
Final Scorecard Release February 17, 2026
Last Date to Download Scorecard March 1, 2026 (6:00 PM)

Direct Link to Check SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Candidates can access the SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 through the direct link on the SSC portal under the “Result/Marks” section after logging in. The portal may redirect from legacy links, but use ssc.gov.in primarily.

SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 – Direct Link (Active)
(Login required at ssc.gov.in for marks download)

Step-by-Step Guide to Download SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  1. Visit the official Staff Selection Commission website: ssc.gov.in.
  2. Click on the “Login” or “Candidate Login” button on the homepage.
  3. Enter your registration number/username, password, and captcha (if prompted).
  4. After logging in, navigate to the “Result/Marks” tab on your dashboard.
  5. Select “Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, NIA and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination 2025” from the list.
  6. Click on the “SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025” or “Marks” link to view your details.
  7. Your final scorecard will display on screen - review marks, cut-off comparison, and status.
  8. Download the PDF and print it for future reference (e.g., document verification). Save it promptly, as access ends after the deadline.
  9. (Use the direct link mentioned above to reach the login faster if available.)

Details Mentioned on SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Your scorecard typically includes:

  • Candidate’s name, roll number, registration number
  • Category/sub-category (UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, etc.)
  • Exam name and applied post
  • Section-wise marks (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & General Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
  • Raw and normalized marks
  • Total marks in CBT (considered for final merit after qualifying PET/PST and medical)
  • Qualifying status (selected/not selected) and any remarks
  • Note on limited availability window

SSC GD Final Cut Off and Merit List 2025

The SSC GD final cut off 2025 was released with the final result on January 15, 2026, and is force-wise, state-wise, category-wise, and gender-wise. It appears in the official result PDF write-up.

Your scorecard marks should be compared to the relevant cut-off for your category/force/state to assess selection. The merit list PDF lists roll numbers of provisionally selected candidates (around 50,047 selected against vacancies), while the scorecard provides individual marks for those who reached physical/medical stages.

(Refer to the official SSC result PDFs for exact cut-off tables - examples range from ~104 to 152+ depending on category/force.)

What to Do After Downloading SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

Selected candidates: Keep multiple printouts/PDFs of the scorecard and final result for document verification, joining, and force allocation.

Verify details (name, roll number, category, etc.) - report serious discrepancies to SSC regional offices promptly.

Non-selected candidates: Use marks for performance analysis and preparation for future SSC exams.

Download and save copies early - the window closes after the last date.

SSC GD Selection Process 2025 (Short Overview)

The process included:

  • Computer-Based Test (CBT): Four sections totaling 160 marks.
  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Qualifying.
  • Detailed Medical Examination (DME)/Review Medical Examination (RME): Qualifying.
  • Final merit based on normalized CBT marks (for qualified candidates), followed by force/state allocation per vacancies and preferences.

The final scorecard reflects CBT performance post-qualifying stages, available briefly post-result.

Important Links for SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025

  • SSC GD Final Scorecard 2025 Download: ssc.gov.in (login to Result/Marks)
  • SSC GD Final Result 2025 Merit List PDF: Available on ssc.gov.in under Results
  • SSC GD Final Cut Off 2025 PDF: In final result write-up on ssc.gov.in
  • SSC Official Website: ssc.gov.in

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Combined Graduate Level Examination Notification 2026 on 21 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 12,256 Group B and Group C posts across various Ministries and Departments. Candidates within the age range as specified in the official notification and holding a Graduation Degree are eligible to apply online at https://ssc.gov.in. The application process will be active from 21 May 2026 to 22 June 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

SSC CGL 2026 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Block No. 12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003, has issued the official notice for Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 vide F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026.The notification includes complete information on vacancies, eligibility, age limits, reservation, examination scheme, syllabus, exam centres, application process, and candidate instructions. Applicants should read the official notice carefully before submitting the form.

  • Download SSC CGL 2026 Official Notification PDF

SSC CGL 2026 Overview

SSC is conducting Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026 for approximately 12,256 vacancies in Group B and Group C posts under different Ministries, Departments, and Government of India organizations. The application process is online only, and the last date to submit the application form is 22 June 2026.

Organization Name Staff Selection Commission (SSC), New Delhi
Examination Name Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 (SSC CGL 2026)
Advertisement No. F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026
Post Name Various Group B (Gazetted/Non-Gazetted) and Group C posts including Assistant Section Officer, Inspector (IT/CE), Sub Inspector (CBI/NIA), Junior Statistical Officer, Assistant Audit Officer, Tax Assistant, Auditor, Accountant, Postal Assistant, and more
Total Vacancies Approximately 12,256 (tentative; updated vacancies will be on https://ssc.gov.in)
Pay Scale Pay Level 4 (Rs. 25,500–81,100) to Pay Level 8 (Rs. 47,600–1,51,100) depending on the post
Application Mode Online only at https://ssc.gov.in or via mY SSC mobile app
Official Website https://ssc.gov.in

SSC CGL 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Notification 21-05-2026
Opening of Online Applications 21-05-2026
Last Date for Submission of Online Applications 22-06-2026 (23:00 hours)
Last Date for Online Fee Payment 23-06-2026 (23:00 hours)
Window for Application Form Correction (with online payment) 29-06-2026 to 01-07-2026 (23:00 hours)
Age Calculated As On 01-08-2026
Educational Qualification Cut-off Date 01-08-2026
Tier-I Computer Based Examination (Tentative) August – September, 2026
Tier-II Computer Based Examination (Tentative) December, 2026

SSC CGL 2026 – Post-wise Vacancy Details

A total of approximately 12,256 vacancies are available across Group B and Group C posts spread over multiple Pay Levels. Post-wise and category-wise vacancies will be updated on the SSC website. State-wise/Zone-wise vacancies are not collected by the Commission; candidates may approach the concerned User Departments for such details.

Pay Level 8 (Rs. 47,600–1,51,100) – Group B Gazetted (Non-Ministerial)

Once a post is allotted, no change will be made due to non-fulfilment of any post-specific physical/medical/educational standard. Some posts (Inspector CE/Examiner/Preventive Officer, Sub Inspector in CBI/NIA/CBN, SI in NCB) have specific Physical Standards, Physical Tests, and Medical Standards as per Annexure-XV & XVI of the notification.

SSC CGL 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India, or subjects/persons of specified neighboring and migrated countries as permitted under Government of India rules, with eligibility certificate wherever required.
Essential Educational Qualification Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent for most posts, as on 01-08-2026.
Junior Statistical Officer (JSO) Bachelor's Degree in any subject with at least 60% marks in Mathematics at 12th level; or Bachelor's Degree with Statistics as one of the subjects at degree level.
Statistical Investigator Grade-II Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Demography, Population Studies, Operation Research, IT, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, or Artificial Intelligence.
Assistant Audit Officer / Assistant Accounts Officer Bachelor's Degree is essential. Desirable qualifications include CA, CMA, CS, M.Com, MBA (Finance), or M.B.E.
Appearing Candidates Final year candidates may apply, but they must possess the essential qualification on or before 01-08-2026.
Distance Learning Degrees Accepted only if approved by Distance Education Bureau, UGC, for Central Government employment. Proof must be produced at document verification.
BRO Posts Only male candidates are eligible for vacancies reported in Border Roads Organization.

SSC CGL 2026 – Age Limit

Age will be calculated as on 01-08-2026. The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or Secondary Examination Certificate will be treated as final.

Age Limit (Post-wise) Date of Birth Range (Both Inclusive)
18 to 27 years 02-08-1999 to 01-08-2008
20 to 30 years 02-08-1996 to 01-08-2006
18 to 30 years 02-08-1996 to 01-08-2008
18 to 32 years (JSO) 02-08-1994 to 01-08-2008

Age Relaxation

Code Category Relaxation Beyond Upper Age Limit
01 SC / ST 5 years
02 OBC 3 years
03 PwBD (General) 10 years
04 PwBD (OBC) 13 years
05 PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years
06 Ex-Servicemen 3 years after deduction of military service from actual age
08 Defence Personnel disabled in operations (General) 3 years
09 Defence Personnel disabled in operations (SC/ST) 8 years
10 Central Govt. Civilian Employees with 3+ years regular service (Group C posts only) Up to 40 years of age
11 Central Govt. Civilian Employees SC/ST with 3+ years regular service (Group C posts only) Up to 45 years of age
12 Widows / Divorced Women / Women judicially separated and not remarried Up to 35 years of age
13 Widows / Divorced Women / Women judicially separated SC/ST and not remarried Up to 40 years of age

SSC CGL 2026 – Application Fee

Category Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male) Rs. 100/- (Non-Refundable)
Women Candidates (All categories) NIL (Exempted)
SC / ST NIL (Exempted)
PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) NIL (Exempted)
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) eligible for reservation NIL (Exempted)

Fee must be paid online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Visa, Mastercard, Maestro, and RuPay debit cards. Fee once paid will not be refunded, and applications without required fee payment will be rejected.

Correction Window Fee: Rs. 200/- for first correction and Rs. 500/- for second correction. This applies to all candidates, including fee-exempt applicants.

SSC CGL 2026 – Selection Process

Selection is through a two-tier Computer Based Examination (CBE). Marks scored in CBE conducted in multiple shifts will be normalized as per the procedure published by SSC vide Notice dated 02-06-2025. There is no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores at any stage.

Tier-I: Computer Based Examination

Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time
A. General Intelligence & Reasoning 25 50 1 hour (Sectional timer: 15 min per section)
1 hour 20 min for scribe-eligible PwBD candidates
B. General Awareness 25 50
C. Quantitative Aptitude 25 50
D. English Comprehension 25 50
Total 100 200  

Tier-I consists of Objective Type / MCQ questions, set in English and Hindi (except English Comprehension). Negative marking: 0.50 marks per wrong answer.

Tier-II: Computer Based Examination

Tier-II is conducted in Paper-I, Paper-II, and Paper-III in separate shifts/days.

Paper Section / Subject Questions Marks Duration
Paper-I (Compulsory for all posts) Section-I: Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning & General Intelligence 30+30 = 60 180 Session-I: 2 hrs 15 min; Section-I: 1 hr timer
Section-II: English Language & Comprehension + General Awareness 45+25 = 70 210 1 hr timer
Section-III: Computer Knowledge Test (qualifying, mandatory) 20 60 15 min
Section-IV: Data Entry Speed Test (DEST) (qualifying, mandatory) - - Session-II: 15 min
Paper-II (JSO / Statistical Investigator only) Statistics 100 200 2 hours
Paper-III (AAO / AAcO only) General Studies (Finance and Economics) 100 200 2 hours

Negative marking in Tier-II: 1 mark per wrong answer in Section-I, II, III of Paper-I; 0.50 marks in Paper-II and Paper-III. Section-III (CKT) and Section-IV (DEST) are qualifying in nature. Candidates must qualify all sections of Paper-I separately. Higher standards of CKT and DEST are set for specific posts (CSS, MEA, AFHQ, SFIO, CBIC, Postal etc.). Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available for PwBD candidates eligible for scribe.

SSC CGL 2026 – Scheme of Examination & Syllabus (Brief)

Paper / Section Key Topics
Tier-I: General Intelligence & Reasoning Analogies, similarities, space visualization, problem solving, arithmetic reasoning, number series, coding-decoding, Venn diagrams, embedded figures, critical thinking, emotional intelligence, social intelligence
Tier-I: General Awareness Current events, India and neighbouring countries, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Policy, Scientific Research
Tier-I: Quantitative Aptitude Whole numbers, decimals, fractions, percentages, ratio, averages, SI/CI, profit & loss, discount, time & work, time & distance, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, bar charts, pie charts
Tier-I: English Comprehension Grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, writing ability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-I: Mathematical Abilities Number systems, arithmetic, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, statistics & probability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-II: English Language & Comprehension Vocabulary, grammar, synonyms, antonyms, idioms, sentence improvement, comprehension passages (current affairs + editorial based)
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-III: Computer Knowledge Computer basics, MS Office (Word/Excel/PowerPoint), Internet & e-mail, networking & cybersecurity basics
Tier-II Paper-II: Statistics Data collection, frequency distribution, central tendency, dispersion, correlation, regression, probability, sampling theory, statistical inference, ANOVA, time series, index numbers
Tier-II Paper-III: General Studies (Finance & Economics) Part A (Finance & Accounts, 80 marks): Financial accounting, GAAP, books of accounts, depreciation, bank reconciliation. Part B (Economics & Governance, 120 marks): Economic concepts, Indian economy, governance, financial markets, budget basics

SSC CGL 2026 – Exam Centres

The examination will be conducted through 9 SSC Regions covering all states and union territories. Candidates must choose three examination centres, in order of priority, within the same region.

Central Region: Agra, Lucknow, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Patna, Gaya Eastern Region: Kolkata, Ranchi, Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, Gangtok Karnataka Kerala Region: Bengaluru, Kochi, Kozhikode, Mysuru Madhya Pradesh Region: Bhopal, Indore, Jabalpur, Raipur North Eastern Region: Guwahati, Imphal, Shillong, Aizawl, Agartala Northern Region: Delhi, Jaipur, Dehradun North Western Region: Chandigarh, Shimla, Jammu, Srinagar, Amritsar Southern Region: Chennai, Hyderabad, Vijayawada, Coimbatore, Madurai Western Region: Mumbai, Pune, Ahmedabad, Surat, Nagpur, Panaji

No request for change of centre will be entertained after submission of the application. SSC may add, cancel, or shift candidates to another centre depending on administrative requirements.

Competition Level Analysis for SSC CGL 2026

The overall competition level for SSC CGL 2026 is expected to remain very high because the examination attracts a massive number of graduate applicants every year across India. Even with around 12,256 vacancies, the effective competition ratio remains extremely tough for high-demand posts.

  1. Applicant volume is huge: SSC CGL is among the most sought-after central government exams, and premium posts receive intense preference from top scorers.
  2. Tier-I acts as the main screening stage: With negative marking and sectional timing, both speed and accuracy matter. Candidates targeting top posts generally need a very strong Tier-I score.
  3. Tier-II gets post-specific: Specialized posts like JSO and AAO attract a smaller but more qualified pool, making domain preparation more important than raw competition numbers.
  4. DEST and CKT can eliminate candidates: Although qualifying in nature, poor performance in these sections can disqualify otherwise strong candidates for certain posts.
  5. Physical standards create an extra filter: Certain Inspector and Sub Inspector posts require physical and medical fitness standards that are checked separately by user departments.

Who Should NOT Apply for SSC CGL 2026?

  1. Candidates who will not possess a Bachelor's Degree by 01-08-2026.
  2. Candidates who fall outside the age limit for all eligible posts even after applicable relaxation.
  3. OBC Creamy Layer candidates intending to claim OBC reservation.
  4. Candidates who cannot meet post-specific physical or medical standards for the posts they plan to prefer.
  5. Non-matriculate Ex-Servicemen or those who have not completed the required length of service.
  6. Candidates applying for JSO without the required Mathematics or Statistics qualification.
  7. Female candidates applying specifically for BRO vacancies, where only male candidates are eligible.
  8. Distance-learning degree holders without valid UGC-DEB approval for the relevant period.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying SSC CGL 2026

  1. Not generating a fresh OTR on the new SSC website.
  2. Using a pre-existing photograph instead of live photo capture where required.
  3. Uploading blurred or wrongly sized signature files.
  4. Selecting the wrong reservation category without valid supporting certificate.
  5. Ignoring post-specific standards before locking post preferences.
  6. Choosing JSO or AAO related preferences without being ready for Paper-II or Paper-III.
  7. Skipping Aadhaar-based authentication even when eligible.
  8. Waiting until the final date for application submission.
  9. Missing the answer key objection window after examination.
  10. Confusing the correction window fee structure.

How to Apply for SSC CGL 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://ssc.gov.in or through the mY SSC mobile application (available on Store). No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates who have not generated OTR on the new website must do so first. Once OTR is generated on the new website, it remains valid for all future examinations on that portal.

Candidates are advised to opt for Aadhaar-based authentication during OTR. Aadhaar-authenticated candidates get significant exemptions: their applications are not rejected for photograph/signature standards, and they need not carry photo ID at the examination venue.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Generate One-Time Registration (OTR) at https://ssc.gov.in (mandatory; use Aadhaar-based authentication if possible)
  2. Complete OTR profile and lock it
  3. Fill the Common Application Form (CAF) including live photograph capture via camera
  4. Upload scanned signature (JPEG/JPG, 10–20 KB, 6.0 cm × 2.0 cm)
  5. Fill the Examination Specific Module (post preferences, exam centre choices - 3 centres within same region in order of priority)
  6. Pay application fee of Rs. 100/- online (if applicable) by 23-06-2026
  7. Preview/Print the application to verify all details before final submission
  8. Use the correction window (29-06 to 01-07-2026) if any correction is needed (chargeable)
  9. Keep printout of application and all documents for at least 3 years

Documents to Keep Ready for Document Verification (after final selection):

  • Matriculation/Secondary School Certificate (proof of date of birth)
  • Mark sheets for all years/semesters of Graduation + Provisional/Degree Certificate
  • SC/ST Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure of the notification)
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure-X); must be valid as on closing date of application
  • EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026–27 (issued based on income for FY 2025–26) in prescribed format (Annexure-XI)
  • PwBD Disability Certificate from competent authority specifying type of disability (as per Annexure-XII to XIII)
  • Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate and other relevant ESM documents
  • Equivalence certificate (for equivalent qualifications other than standard degrees)
  • DEB/UGC approval letter (for Distance Learning degree holders)
  • Certificate of proficiency in State Regional/official language (for AAO/Assistant Audit Officer State Cadre)

Apply Online at: https://ssc.gov.in | Toll Free Helpline: 1800 309 3063

Important Instructions for SSC CGL 2026 Candidates

  • Final allotment is based on merit-cum-preference, and no alternate post will be offered if a candidate fails post-specific standards after allotment.
  • OBC candidates claiming reservation must not belong to the creamy layer as on the closing date of application.
  • EWS certificate must be valid for FY 2026–27 and should be based on income for FY 2025–26.
  • PwBD candidates must choose the correct sub-category in the application form because no later change is allowed.
  • Scribe rules are strict: the scribe must complete OTR, be Aadhaar-authenticated, and cannot assist more than one candidate.
  • Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available to eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Tentative answer keys can be challenged online by paying Rs. 100 per question within the specified period only.
  • No re-evaluation or re-checking of scores is available at any stage.
  • Candidates should preserve the application form and related documents for at least 3 years.
  • Candidature remains provisional until certificates and documents are verified by the Appointing Authority.
  • Fraudulent claims of reservation status may lead to debarment from all SSC examinations.
  • Any update in exam schedule or process will be communicated through the SSC website only.
  • Applications without the required fee, unless exempted, or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) have officially announced the Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) Recruitment 2026 with 11,127 openings. The detailed notification was published on 14 May 2026. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18 to 30 years and hold Matriculation + ITI or Diploma/Degree in Engineering can now submit their online applications from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Railway Recruitment Boards have officially released Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) No. 01/2026 for recruitment to 11127 posts of Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) under various Railway Zones, Government of India. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination syllabus, medical standards, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification and its annexures before applying online. The notification PDF is available on all official RRB websites.

  • Download RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), Ministry of Railways, Government of India
Advertisement No. CEN No. 01/2026
Post Name Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP)
Department Ministry of Railways, Government of India (Various Zonal Railways)
Total Vacancies 11127 (All RRBs combined)
Pay Scale Level-2 in 7th CPC Pay Matrix; Initial Pay Rs. 19,900/-
Medical Standard A-1 (Stringent vision and physical fitness standards; Lasik surgery candidates NOT eligible)
Application Mode Online only (via official RRB websites)
Selection Mode CBT-1 (75 marks) + CBT-2 (Part A: 100 marks + Part B: 75 marks qualifying) + CBAT + Document Verification + Medical Examination
Suitability for PwBD Not Suitable
Official Website Refer RRB-wise websites listed below (e.g., www.rrbald.gov.in, www.rrbmumbai.gov.in, etc.)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Indicative Notice in Employment News 21/03/2026
Opening of Online Application Portal 15-05-2026 (00:00 Hrs.)
Last Date for Online Application Submission 14-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Last Date for Application Fee Payment 16-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Modification Window for Application Corrections 17-06-2026 to 26-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Age Calculated As On 01-07-2026
CBT-1 / CBT-2 / CBAT Date To be notified on official RRB websites

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 11127 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant Loco Pilot across all Railway Recruitment Boards and Zonal Railways. These are distributed across UR, SC, ST, OBC, and EWS categories. Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) vacancies are included within the total vacancy count and are not separately additional. The post is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD).

Category No. of Posts
UR (Unreserved) 4860
SC (Scheduled Caste) 1746
ST (Scheduled Tribe) 919
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 2598
EWS (Economically Weaker Section) 1004
Total 11127
Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) - included in above total 1115

RRB & Zone-wise Vacancy Details (Selected RRBs)

RRB Zone UR SC ST OBC EWS Total ExSM
Ahmedabad WR 62 22 11 38 14 147 15
Ajmer NWR + WCR 318 115 59 174 52 718 72
Mumbai CR + SCR + WR 336 219 106 380 152 1193 120
Bhubaneswar ECoR 548 70 36 48 99 801 80
Guwahati NFR 272 109 63 164 68 676 68
Secunderabad ECoR + SCR 625 209 102 390 94 1420 143
Ranchi ECR + SER 472 233 105 363 127 1300 130
All RRBs Total 4860 1746 919 2598 1004 11127 1115

Note: Complete RRB & Zone-wise vacancy details are available in Annexure B of the official notification.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (14-06-2026). Educational/Technical qualification certificates must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute. Candidates awaiting results of their final examination for the prescribed qualification are NOT eligible to apply. The post of ALP is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD). Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for this post.

Educational Qualification

Option Details
Option A - Matriculation + ITI Matriculation / SSLC plus ITI from recognised institutions of NCVT/SCVT in the trades of Fitter, Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV), Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Wireman, Tractor Mechanic, Armature & Coil Winder, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning Mechanic. OR Matriculation / SSLC plus Course Completed Act Apprenticeship in the above trades.
Option B - Matriculation + Diploma/Degree in Engineering Matriculation / SSLC plus 3-year Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Electronics / Automobile Engineering (or combination of these streams) from a recognised institution in lieu of ITI. Note: Degree in the above Engineering disciplines will also be acceptable in lieu of Diploma.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be between 18 and 30 years of age as on 01-07-2026. Age relaxation is available for various categories as detailed below. If a candidate is eligible for relaxation on more than one ground, only the maximum relaxation will be granted (not cumulative). SC, ST, and OBC (NCL) candidates applying against UR vacancies will not get any age relaxation. There is no fixed limit on the number of attempts - candidates can appear as long as they are within the prescribed upper age limit.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) & EWS 18 to 30 years (as on 01-07-2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
SC & ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
Ex-Servicemen (UR & EWS) 3 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (OBC-NCL) 6 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (SC & ST) 8 years after deduction of length of service from age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - UR & EWS Maximum 40 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - OBC (NCL) Maximum 43 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - SC & ST Maximum 45 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (UR & EWS) Maximum 35 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (OBC-NCL) Maximum 38 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (SC & ST) Maximum 40 years of age

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the non-refundable recruitment fee online. Fee can be paid only through online mode - internet banking, debit/credit cards, or UPI. Applications received without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected. Candidates who appear in CBT-1 will receive a refund of their examination fee (after deducting applicable bank charges) credited to their Aadhaar-seeded bank account. Candidates whose applications are rejected, or who do not appear in CBT-1, will not receive any refund.

Category Examination Fee Refund on Appearing in CBT-1
All Candidates (except categories below) Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred only) Rs. 400/- refunded (deducting bank charges)
SC / ST / Ex-Servicemen / Female / Transgender / Minorities / Economically Backward Class (EBC) Rs. 250/- (Rupees Two Hundred and Fifty only) Rs. 250/- fully refunded (deducting bank charges)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 consists of five successive stages - First Stage CBT (CBT-1), Second Stage CBT (CBT-2), Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT), Document Verification (DV), and Medical Examination (ME). CBT-1 is a screening exam only and its marks are not counted in the final panel. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of combined marks from Part-A of CBT-2 (50% weightage) and CBAT scores (50% weightage). Negative marking of 1/3rd mark per wrong answer applies in both CBT-1 and CBT-2. There is no negative marking in CBAT.

Stage 1 - First Stage CBT (CBT-1) - Screening Only

Details Particulars
Duration 60 Minutes
Total Questions / Marks 75 Questions / 75 Marks (1 mark per question)
Type Objective MCQ - Computer Based Test
Negative Marking 1/3rd mark deducted per wrong answer
Minimum Qualifying Marks UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Shortlisting for CBT-2 15 times the vacancies notified per RRB (based on CBT-1 merit, RRB-wise)
Syllabus Mathematics, Mental Ability (Reasoning), General Science (10th standard level)

Stage 2 - Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)

Part Duration Questions Nature Minimum Qualifying Marks
Part-A 90 Minutes 100 Questions / 100 Marks Competitive - marks counted for merit UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Part-B 60 Minutes 75 Questions / 75 Marks Qualifying only - trade-specific 35% for all candidates irrespective of category
Total Duration 175 Questions / 2.5 Hours  

Part-A Syllabus (CBT-2): Mathematics, General Intelligence and Reasoning, Basic Science and Engineering (Engineering Drawing, Units, Measurements, Basic Electricity, Levers, IT Literacy, etc.)

Part-B Syllabus (CBT-2) - Trade-wise Qualifying Test:

Sl. No. Qualification of Candidate Trade / Subject of Choice for Part-B
1 Electrical Engineering (and combinations) Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Wireman, Armature & Coil Winder, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic
2 Electronics Engineering (and combinations) Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV)
3 Mechanical Engineering (and combinations) Fitter, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic, Heat Engine, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic
4 Automobile Engineering (and combinations) Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic

Stage 3 - Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)

Shortlisting for CBAT 8 times the ALP vacancies per community/category per RRB (based on Part-A marks of CBT-2)
Qualifying Marks Minimum T-score of 42 in each test battery separately (all candidates irrespective of category)
Language English and Hindi only
Negative Marking No negative marking

Stage 4 & 5 - Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (ME)

Final merit list is prepared RRB-wise from candidates qualifying in CBAT, with 50% weightage to Part-A of CBT-2 marks and 50% weightage to CBAT scores. Candidates equal to the number of vacancies are shortlisted for DV and Medical Examination. In case of a tie, the older candidate is preferred; if age is also equal, alphabetical order of given name (A to Z) is applied.

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Seniority of age (older candidate preferred)
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order (A to Z) of given name (not surname)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Medical Standards

The post of ALP carries Medical Standard A-1. Candidates must be physically fit in all respects with stringent vision requirements. Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible. A Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Part-A signed by General Physician and Part-B signed by Eye Specialist, both from a Central/State Government Hospital or Medical College) must be submitted on the CBAT exam day.

Medical Standard General Fitness Vision Standards
A-1 Physically fit in all respects (i) Distant Vision: 6/6, 6/6 without glasses with fogging test (must not accept +2D); (ii) Near Vision: Sn: 0.6, 0.6 without glasses; (iii) Must pass tests for Colour Vision, Binocular Vision, Field of Vision, Night Vision, Mesopic Vision, etc.

How to Apply for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official websites of participating RRBs (listed at Para 13.7 of the CEN). Hard copy / offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal is active from 15-05-2026 to 14-06-2026 (23:59 Hrs.). Candidates must first create an account on the RRB portal. Those who created an account for CENs notified in 2024/2025 should use the same credentials. Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and the chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation. Candidates can apply to only one RRB and submit only one application - multiple applications to the same or different RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and debarment from future RRB/RRC exams. Aadhaar or DigiLocker authentication is strongly advised during account creation for a smoother recruitment process. A live photograph is captured during the online application; pre-existing photographs are not accepted. No documents need to be uploaded at application stage (except signature and SC/ST certificate for free travel pass, if applicable).

Documents to be Produced at Document Verification (on being called):

  • Age proof - Matriculation / SSLC / 10th class certificate
  • Educational qualifications (Matriculation onwards) - marksheets and certificates
  • ITI / Diploma / Degree certificate in prescribed trade / discipline
  • SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / Ex-Servicemen certificate (if applicable), in prescribed format and issued by competent authority
  • Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Annexure VIII A & VIII B) - to be submitted on CBAT day
  • NOC from present employer (if applicable, for Government servants)

Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official RRB website for their chosen RRB for updates on all stages. Helpline for technical queries: Email: [email protected] | Phone: 0172-565-3333 and 9592001188 (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).

Apply Online at: Visit the official website of your chosen RRB (see list below)

Important Instructions for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate is accepted - multiple applications to one or more RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and permanent debarment from RRB/RRC exams.
  • Candidates awaiting results of their final prescribed qualification examination are NOT eligible to apply.
  • Candidates who have undergone Lasik or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP.
  • Selection of RRB, once submitted, is final and cannot be changed at any later date.
  • Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation.
  • Examination fee is refundable (after deducting bank charges) only to candidates who appear in CBT-1; rejected or absent candidates are not eligible for refund.
  • Refund will be credited only to the Aadhaar-seeded bank account; candidates must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to an active bank account.
  • Mobile phones, electronic gadgets, watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, calculators, books, pen, food items, and all other listed banned items are strictly prohibited inside the test centre.
  • Examination centre, date, and shift allotted in the e-call letter are final - no requests for change will be entertained under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must maintain identical signatures throughout all stages of the recruitment process. No BLOCK/CAPITAL/DISJOINTED letter signatures are acceptable.
  • Free Sleeper Class Railway Pass is available for SC/ST candidates for CBT/CBAT/DV/Medical Examination stages (if opted in application).
  • Candidates are advised to visit only official RRB websites and beware of fake websites, unscrupulous agents, and touts claiming to provide Railway jobs through influence or unfair means. RRBs never appoint agents or coaching centres for recruitment.

SBI 7150 Apprentice Online Form 2026

📅 Updated: 24 May 2026 ✍️ By: OnlyJobs Editorial Team 🌐 Source: Official Notification

Check complete details for SBI 7150 Apprentice Online Form 2026. Includes important dates, application link, eligibility, syllabus and result info — updated live on OnlyJobs.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest State Bank of India (SBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 19-05-2026 at the official website of SBI, this new recruitment drive offers 7150 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 19-05-2026 to 08-06-2026. Find all details about the SBI Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 7150 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official SBI website.

  • Download SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

SBI is recruiting 7150 Apprentice posts with Any Graduate qualification, last date 08 June 2026.

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), State Bank of India
Total Training Seats 7150 (across 34 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)
Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)
Duration of Training 1 Year (On-the-Job Training)
Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + SBI website)
Selection Mode Online Written Test + Test of Local Language + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in)
Online Examination Tentatively in July 2026
Official Website https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 19/05/2026
Opening of Online Registration of Application 19-05-2026
Last Date for Online Registration of Application 08-06-2026
Age Calculated As On 01-04-2026
Call Letter / Acquaint Yourself Booklet Download Tentatively in June 2026
Online Written Examination Date Tentatively in July 2026
Local Language Test Date To be notified after Online Written Examination result

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 7150 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only.

State / UT Language Total
Gujarat Gujarati 217
Andhra Pradesh Telugu / Urdu 709
Karnataka Kannada 276
Chhattisgarh Hindi 93
Madhya Pradesh Hindi 285
Odisha Odia 229
UT Ladakh Ladakhi / Urdu / Bhoti 15
Himachal Pradesh Hindi 180
UT Chandigarh Hindi / Punjabi 15

Keralam Malayalam 403
Total   7150

Abbreviations: SC – Scheduled Caste, ST – Scheduled Tribe, OBC – Other Backward Classes, EWS – Economically Weaker Section, UR – Unreserved, VI – Visually Impaired, HI – Hearing Impaired, LD – Locomotor Disability, d&e – Autism Spectrum Disorder, Specific Learning Disorder, Mental Illness, and Multiple Disabilities. Reservation for PwBD is horizontal within overall vacancies. Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Categories Eligible

Category Description
Visual Impairment (VI) Blindness (total absence of sight / visual acuity less than 3/60 in better eye / field less than 10°) and Low Vision (visual acuity not exceeding 6/18 / field less than 40°)
Hearing Impaired (HI) Hard of Hearing – 60 DB to 70 DB hearing loss in speech frequencies in both ears
Locomotor Disability (LD) OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Muscular Dystrophy, Acid Attack Victims, Spinal Deformity (SD) and Spinal Injury (SI) without associated neurological/limb dysfunction
d & e Categories Autism Spectrum Disorder (Mild); Multiple Disabilities among LD, VI, HI, SLD, MI & ASD

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility (01-04-2026). Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online through SBI website (https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers).
Educational Qualification (as on 01-04-2026) Graduation from a recognized University / Institute.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship either in SBI or in any other organization earlier, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training as per the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after attainment of educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
CIBIL Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid outstanding till date of issuance of offer of engagement shall not be eligible.
Aadhaar Requirement All candidates (except those belonging to Northeastern States where alternate ID proof will do) should produce Aadhaar Number before engagement, i.e., contract signing for apprenticeship training.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-04-2026, i.e., candidates must have been born not later than 01-04-2006 and not earlier than 02-04-1998 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
PwBD As per Government of India guidelines

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date for payment of fees. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
General / OBC / EWS Rs. 300/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD NIL

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on (i) Online Written Test and (ii) Test of Local Language, followed by Medical Examination. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, qualifying in local language test, and medical fitness. A Wait List (State-category wise) will be maintained for one year from the date of result or next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. Candidates will be released only once from this waitlist against non-joining subject to availability of qualified candidates.

Stage 1 - Online Written Examination (Objective Type)

Sl. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
2 General English 25 25 15 minutes English only
3 Quantitative Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
4 Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
Total 100 100 60 minutes  

Each test has a separate timing as mentioned above. There will be NEGATIVE MARKS for wrong answers — 1/4th of the mark assigned for the question will be deducted for each wrong answer. Test questions (except General English) will be set in 13 Regional Languages viz. Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu — in addition to English and Hindi — as applicable State-wise. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order; tie-break is by age in descending order.

Medium of Examination – State/UT-wise

Sr. State/UT Medium of Exam Sr. State/UT Medium of Exam
1 Andaman & Nicobar English, Hindi 18 Maharashtra English, Hindi, Marathi
2 Andhra Pradesh English, Hindi, Telugu, Urdu 19 Manipur English, Hindi, Manipuri
3 Arunachal Pradesh English, Hindi 20 Meghalaya English, Hindi
4 Assam English, Hindi, Assamese, Bengali 21 Mizoram English, Hindi
5 Bihar English, Hindi 22 Nagaland English, Hindi
6 Chandigarh English, Hindi, Punjabi 23 Odisha English, Hindi, Odia
7 Chhattisgarh English, Hindi 24 Puducherry English, Hindi, Tamil
8 Goa English, Hindi, Konkani 25 Punjab English, Hindi, Punjabi
9 Gujarat English, Hindi, Gujarati 26 Rajasthan English, Hindi
10 Haryana English, Hindi, Punjabi 27 Sikkim English, Hindi
11 Himachal Pradesh English, Hindi 28 Tamil Nadu English, Hindi, Tamil
12 Jammu & Kashmir English, Hindi, Urdu 29 Telangana English, Hindi, Telugu, Urdu
13 Jharkhand English, Hindi 30 Tripura English, Hindi, Bengali
14 Karnataka English, Hindi, Kannada 31 Uttar Pradesh English, Hindi, Urdu
15 Kerala English, Hindi, Malayalam 32 Uttarakhand English, Hindi
16 Ladakh English, Hindi, Urdu 33 West Bengal English, Hindi, Bengali
17 Madhya Pradesh English, Hindi 34 Delhi English, Hindi

Stage 2 - Test of Local Language

The test of specified local language will be conducted after qualifying the online written examination. Candidates who fail to qualify this test will not be engaged as apprentice. Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet/certificate evidencing having studied the specified local language will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates must appear at their own expense; SBI will not reimburse travelling expenses.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank and satisfactory completion of KYE formalities.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Weightage in Junior Associate Recruitment

Weightage/Relaxation to apprentices in recruitment of Junior Associates shall be given as per the recruitment policy of the Bank as amended from time to time, to apprentices who successfully complete the apprenticeship training in SBI, subject to candidate's satisfactory conduct and performance during the entire training period. Successful completion refers to passing of the skill evaluation test (Trade test) and obtention of the joint National Apprenticeship Certificate issued by SBI-NSDC / BFSI-SSC.

Competition Level Analysis for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high due to 7150 seats being offered at the all-India level and the exam being open to any graduate, including those already preparing for bank and SSC exams. While the written test is of easy to moderate difficulty and slightly below SBI Clerk level, the large number of serious aspirants in high-population states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, West Bengal, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana will push state-wise and category-wise cut-offs upward.

In practical terms, each seat is likely to attract dozens to over a hundred candidates in major states, making speed, accuracy, and sectional balance crucial to clearing the single-stage written exam followed by the local language test. However, the effective competition reduces at later stages because some candidates will fail to qualify the language test or be filtered out at the document verification, CIBIL, Aadhaar, and medical fitness stages, and SBI will finally select candidates strictly on state-wise and category-wise merit from those who clear all stages.

Who should NOT apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job in SBI; this is only a 1‑year apprenticeship with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment.
  • Candidates who already completed any Apprenticeship (in SBI or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates outside the age band 20–28 years as on the cut-off date (even 1 day younger/older is not eligible, except notified relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
  • Aspirants who do not have a Graduation degree from a recognised University/Institute by the specified date.
  • Candidates not fluent in the notified local language of the State/UT (reading, writing, speaking, understanding), and who are unlikely to clear the language test or do not have 10th/12th proof.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register on the NAPS portal before applying, as this is a mandatory prerequisite for SBI Apprentice.
  • Candidates with serious CIBIL/loan/credit‑card defaults who have not regularised or repaid outstanding dues by the time of offer; such profiles are normally rejected in bank recruitments.
  • Ex‑Servicemen / Disabled Ex‑Servicemen, where the notification clearly states no engagement / no reservation for them under Apprentice.
  • PwBD candidates whose disability type falls under “likely to improve” (temporary disability) where reservation benefits may not apply as per notification conditions.
  • Candidates who cannot produce valid Aadhaar (except where exempted, e.g., certain North‑Eastern states with alternate ID allowed).

Common mistakes / What to check before applying SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying in a wrong State or language where you cannot comfortably read, write and speak the specified local language.
  • Entering wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed later and mismatch with certificates at DV can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from creamy layer applying as OBC instead of UR; only non‑creamy OBCs with valid, recent certificate get reservation.
  • Uploading blurred/incorrect photo, signature, thumb impression or declaration (wrong size, wrong format, someone else’s handwriting) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using invalid or inactive mobile number/email ID, causing failure to receive OTPs, registration details, call letters or further communication.
  • Submitting multiple applications for different states or with different details; SBI usually accepts only the last complete application and may forfeit extra fees.
  • Ignoring negative marking and over‑attempting in the exam, which can sharply reduce your score despite high attempts.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later protesting about absorption or permanent posting after apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online; no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and read the detailed guidelines on https://bfsissc.com before applying online through the SBI website.

Candidates should have a valid email ID and mobile number which should be kept active till declaration of results. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last date.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded (as per Annexure-I):

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (140×60 pixels; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink (240×240 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) — in candidate's own handwriting
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at the time of online application)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Examination / Document Verification / Engagement:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) along with photo identity proof (Passport / Aadhaar / PAN Card / Driving License / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Identity Card issued by School or College / Gazetted Officer in official letterhead) in original and self-attested photocopy
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST candidates) — to be submitted at the time of engagement if called for
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed Government of India format, issued during the period 01-04-2026 to the date of joining (for OBC candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by Competent Authority in the format prescribed by Government of India, valid for the year 2026-27 (for EWS candidates)
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by a Competent Authority as per Government of India guidelines (for PwBD candidates)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (Graduation onwards)

Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to SBI. No Hard Copy of the Call Letter for Online Written Examination will be sent by post. Candidates must appear for the test at their own cost; SBI will not pay or reimburse any such expenses.

Apply Online at: https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

For queries: Call 022-22820427 (11:00 AM to 06:00 PM on working days) or visit https://cgrs.ibps.in — mention "Engagement of Apprentice in SBI" in the subject.

Important Instructions for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. NOR IS THE SAME CONTRACT EMPLOYMENT. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of SBI and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for employees of the Bank.
  • SBI shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to Apprentices during and/or after the completion of the Apprenticeship period. After completion, candidates will be relieved from the respective work area.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.
  • Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online on SBI website is required.
  • No Ex-Servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an Apprentice and there is no reservation for Ex-Servicemen in the Apprentice Engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General or General (LD/VI/HI/d&e) as applicable.
  • Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.
  • Candidates with CIBIL default record (unpaid loans/credit card dues) are not eligible for engagement until dues are regularized/repaid.
  • All candidates (except Northeastern State candidates) must produce Aadhaar Number before engagement/contract signing.
  • Not more than one application should be submitted; in case of multiple applications, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • PwBD candidates with disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability) category are not eligible for reservation.
  • PwBD candidates eligible for scribe facility: candidates with 40% or more disability having physical limitation to write are eligible. Scribe must be arranged by the candidate at his/her own cost. Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour of examination is allowed (whether availing scribe or not) for eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their registered email ID/mobile active for receiving call letters/advices etc.
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, pagers, earphones or any other electronic/communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only — candidates should visit regularly.
  • No request for change of examination centre shall be entertained. No Travel Expenses will be reimbursed/paid by SBI.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.
  • Bank reserves right to cancel the selection process partly/entirely at any stage.

The latest Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) Recruitment 2026 has been published for Young Professionals at Durgapur Steel Plant (DSP), IISCO Steel Plant (ISP), and Alloy Steels Plant (ASP)! Published on 22-05-2026 vide Advertisement No. ISP/YP/2026/01, this new recruitment drive offers 48 openings for eligible candidates across Projects, Works, and Materials Management Divisions. Applicants who have completed 23 years of age and possess a B.E./B.Tech. degree with minimum 65% marks and relevant post-qualification experience can apply online from 22-05-2026 to 06-06-2026. Find all details about the SAIL Young Professional new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

Durgapur Steel Plant (DSP), IISCO Steel Plant (ISP) & Alloy Steels Plant (ASP) - Plants of Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL), a Maharatna Company - have officially released Advertisement No. ISP/YP/2026/01 dated 22/05/2026 for engagement of 48 Young Professionals in DSP Durgapur, ISP Burnpur, and ASP Durgapur on a fixed-term contractual basis. The notification includes complete details on engagement-wise vacancies, plant-wise distribution, qualification and experience requirements, remuneration, tenure, benefits, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying online at www.sail.co.in.

SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Overview

SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions (qualification and experience) as on the closing date of application i.e. 06-06-2026. All Indian Nationals who have completed 23 years of age and possess the requisite educational/professional qualifications and work-experience can apply. B.E./B.Tech. or equivalent must be Regular/Full time from a Government-recognized University/Institution with minimum 65% marks. Work experience must be in Executive Cadre in a Steel Plant/Process Industry/Manufacturing Industry.

Engagement Code Essential Qualification Desirable Qualification Desired Work Experience

W-6 B.E./B.Tech. in Control & Instrumentation/Instrumentation & Control Engineering/Robotics & Automation/Automation & Robotics M.E./M.Tech./Ph.D. in related discipline Maintenance/troubleshooting and programming of PLC systems (Siemens/ABB/Allen-Bradley/Schneider); drive systems (VFD, AC/DC drives); industrial communication protocols (Profibus, Profinet, Modbus, Ethernet/IP)

Note: Essential qualification of B.E./B.Tech. or equivalent must be Regular/Full time in the respective stream from a Government-recognized University/Institution with minimum 65% marks. For foreign university qualifications, an equivalence certificate from the Competent Authority is required. CGPA scores will be converted to percentage as per University/Institution norms; if no norms are specified, conversion will be proportionate (e.g., 8.3 out of 10 = 83%).

SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Minimum age is 23 years. Maximum age varies by grade - YP-1: 35 years, YP-2: 38 years, YP-3: 42 years. Age is calculated as on the closing date of application i.e. 06-06-2026. No age relaxation details are mentioned in the notification for reserved categories - all eligible Indian Nationals may apply as per the age limit specified for each engagement code.

Grade Minimum Age Upper Age Limit Minimum Post-qualification Experience
Young Professional – 1 (YP-1) 23 years 35 years 3 years
Young Professional – 2 (YP-2) 23 years 38 years 6 years
Young Professional – 3 (YP-3) 23 years 42 years 10 years

SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee (Registration & Processing Fee) is to be paid online and is non-refundable under any circumstances. Candidates are therefore advised to verify their eligibility before paying the fee and fill in payment details carefully. No request for editing of payment details will be entertained in wrong submission cases and candidature will stand rejected.

Category Registration & Processing Fee
Young Professionals – YP-1, YP-2 and YP-3 (All candidates) Rs. 500/- (Non-Refundable) - Online payment mode only

SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Remuneration & Benefits

Young Professionals will receive a monthly consolidated retainership fee with annual increments based on performance. They are NOT entitled to any other perks, allowances, loans/advances, PRP, Provident Fund, Pension, Gratuity, or terminal/service benefits beyond what is specifically mentioned in the advertisement. This is a Contract for Service - not employment under the State.

Grade Monthly Retainership Fee Annual Increment Mobile/Broadband Ceiling Insurance Reimbursement (Max/Year)
Young Professional – 1 (YP-1) Rs. 70,000/- 4%/7%/10% (performance-based) Rs. 800/- per month Up to Rs. 20,000/-
Young Professional – 2 (YP-2) Rs. 1,00,000/- 4%/7%/10% (performance-based) Rs. 1,000/- per month Up to Rs. 25,000/-
Young Professional – 3 (YP-3) Rs. 1,40,000/- 4%/7%/10% (performance-based) Rs. 1,200/- per month Up to Rs. 30,000/-

Other Benefits:

Benefit Details
Accommodation (Outstation YPs) Company accommodation (2-BHK or equivalent) at Rs. 3,000/- per month at plant location (YP-3 may get 3-BHK/equivalent at Rs. 5,000/- per month), subject to availability. No HRA payable. During initial 3 months, SAIL Guest House/Transit House at Rs. 200/- per day (subject to availability).
Medical Facility OPD & IPD at Company-owned hospital at any Plant/Unit location for self, spouse, and two dependent children - free of cost. No medical referral facility.
Self-Insurance (Mandatory) Candidates must avail adequate Medical/Life/Health/Accident insurance. Premium reimbursable up to limits specified above (on production of documentary proof).
Official Tour/Travel (YP-1) Rail – 2AC/CC; Stay up to Rs. 2,000/- per day; Local Travel & food @ Rs. 1,000/- per day (at non-metro locations; 15% higher at metros)
Official Tour/Travel (YP-2) Air (Economy, for distances over 500 km) / Rail – 2AC/CC; Stay up to Rs. 2,500/- per day; Local Travel & food @ Rs. 1,000/- per day
Official Tour/Travel (YP-3) Air (Economy) / Rail – 1AC/EC; Stay up to Rs. 3,000/- per day; Local Travel & food @ Rs. 1,200/- per day
Joining Travel Reimbursement YP-1 & YP-2: 2 AC train fare from nearest Railway Station. YP-3: Airfare (economy)/1 AC train fare from nearest Railway Station/airport.
Interview Travel Reimbursement Single to-and-fro AC-2 Tier Rail fare (with reservation/Tatkal charges) from correspondence address to interview venue by shortest route (if distance more than 30 km each way), on production of proof of journey.
Leave Entitlement 1.5 days per completed month = 18 days per year; no accumulation beyond the calendar year. Absence beyond 18 days in a calendar year is not payable.
Working Hours 8.5 hours per day, 6 days per week; as per allocated shift timing. Young Professionals may be required to work beyond prescribed hours as per work-exigencies at no extra cost.

SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for Young Professional engagements will be based on: (i) Application scrutiny for eligibility; (ii) Computer Based Test (CBT)/Aptitude/General Management/Psychometric Test if applications received are more than 5 times the requirement (shortlisting ratio 1:3 based on test performance); (iii) Personal Interview if applications are fewer than 5 times the requirement - all eligible candidates called for interview. Final merit list is prepared with CBT:Interview weightage of 80:20. A discipline-wise panel (1:1 ratio of selected to waitlisted) will be maintained for 6 months for non-joining/early exit cases.

Stage 1 - Application Scrutiny

All applications received by the closing date (06-06-2026) will be scrutinized for fulfilment of eligibility criteria. Candidature is purely provisional and will be verified against original documents at the time of Interview, Pre-Employment Medical Examination, and Joining.

Stage 2 - Computer Based Test (CBT) / Shortlisting Test (if applicable)

If applications received against any engagement exceed 5 times the requirement, a Computer Based Test/Aptitude/General Management/Psychometric Test or any standard test/exam will be organized. Shortlisting for interview will be in the ratio of 1:3 based on test performance. If the cut-off marks are obtained by more candidates, all of them will be called for interview. CBT carries 80% weightage in the final merit list.

Stage 3 - Personal Interview

Shortlisted/all eligible candidates will be called for personal interview at centres spread across the country. Interview carries 20% weightage in the final merit list. No request for change in date or centre of interview will be entertained. Candidates from PSU/Govt. Departments/Autonomous Bodies must produce NOC in original from the employer at the time of interview.

Stage 4 - Pre-Employment Medical Examination & Joining

Joining of selected candidates is subject to medical fitness. Selected candidates must join within 30 days from the offer of engagement - no extension permitted. Every selected candidate must enter into a Contract for Service agreement with the respective SAIL Plant before joining.

How to Apply for SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026

Eligible and interested candidates must apply online only through SAIL's official Careers website: www.sail.co.in (Careers) or http://sailcareers.com. No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates must first complete One-Time Registration (if new user) and then proceed to fill the application. Once all required details are entered and the application is confirmed, no editing will be allowed. After confirmation, candidates must pay the Registration & Processing Fee online, submit the application, and download the Registration Slip with Application ID.

Documents to be Scanned and Uploaded (PDF format, max 300 KB each):

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph in .jpg/.jpeg format (max 50 KB, white background, no sunglasses/dark glasses)
  • Photograph of running signature in .jpg/.jpeg format (max 20 KB - not capital letters, individual alphabets, or initials)
  • Matriculation certificate showing Date of Birth
  • Pass Certificate of XII, B.E./B.Tech./M.E./M.Tech./Ph.D. or any other relevant qualification
  • Experience certificate(s) showing relevant post-qualification experience from employer
  • NOC from present employer (if employed under PSUs/Autonomous Bodies/Govt. Department)
  • Photo Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Passport, or Voter ID)
  • Address Proof (Voter ID, Driving License, Aadhaar Card or others)
  • CGPA/SGPA to percentage conversion certificate/chart/formulae (if applicable - optional)
  • Equivalence certificate for foreign university qualifications (if applicable)

After applying online, candidates must download the system-generated Registration Slip with Application ID and retain it for future reference. All subsequent communications - CBT Admit Card, Interview call letters, Pre-Employment Medical Examination letter, final selection intimation - will be sent via email/SMS only. SAIL will not be responsible for non-receipt due to wrong/invalid email ID/mobile number.

Apply Online at: https://sailcareers.com/sail2026yp/ 

Important Instructions for SAIL Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This engagement is purely of temporary nature (Contract for Service) and shall NOT create any right for regular appointment on any ground whatsoever. It does NOT create an employer-employee relationship with SAIL.
  • Initial tenure is 3 years, extendable up to 2 additional years (one year at a time) based on satisfactory performance - maximum total period is 5 years.
  • Performance is reviewed annually; tenure extension is at the discretion of the Company based on performance and eligibility.
  • During the first 6 months, either party can terminate with 7 days' notice without notice pay. Beyond 6 months, 1 month's notice or 1 month's retainership fee in lieu is required by either party.
  • Absence for 15 consecutive days without prior written sanction may lead to immediate termination without notice.
  • Self-insurance (Medical/Life/Health/Accident) is mandatory for all Young Professionals.
  • No other perks, allowances, loans, advances, PRP, PF, Pension, Gratuity, or terminal benefits are payable beyond those specifically mentioned in the advertisement.
  • In the event of death, injury or illness attributable to performance of services, neither the Young Professional nor family members shall be entitled to any compensation from SAIL.
  • Candidates must join within 30 days from the offer of engagement - no extension is permitted on any grounds.
  • Every selected candidate must sign a Contract for Service agreement with the respective SAIL Plant before joining. Engagement without a signed agreement is null and void.
  • Candidates must furnish an undertaking/declaration regarding good moral character and absence of criminal conviction or pending judicial proceedings at the time of joining.
  • SAIL reserves the right to carry out pre-engagement verification of antecedents and credentials including general character. Adverse reports may lead to termination without notice.
  • Examination centres allotted are binding - no request for change of examination centre will be entertained.
  • Court of jurisdiction for any dispute will be at Asansol/Durgapur, West Bengal.
  • SAIL-ISP/DSP/ASP reserves the right to cancel/restrict/modify/alter the engagement process at any stage without notice or reason, without any liability to compensate applicants.
  • All corrigendum/addendum/notices will be published only on www.sail.co.in - candidates must check the website periodically.
