PwBD candidates with disability not less than 40% are eligible for reservation/relaxation where posts are reserved for them, and for other relaxations where posts are identified as suitable for them.

Post-wise Essential Qualifications Summary

Post Essential Qualification Essential Experience
Airworthiness Officer (DGCA) Bachelor's degree in Physics/Mathematics/Aircraft Maintenance or Engineering Degree in Aeronautical/Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics/Telecommunication from recognized university; AND valid AME License in Category B1 or B2 issued by DGCA (Aeronautical Engineering degree holders may be considered without AME License) Minimum 3 years of Aircraft Maintenance experience on operating aircraft in DGCA-approved Aircraft Maintenance Organization
Deputy Controller of Explosive (PESO) Degree in Chemical Engineering/Technology OR Master's Degree in Chemistry from recognized University/Institute 3 years' experience in Chemical works or manufacture/handling of explosives and/or compressed gases and/or refining of Petroleum
Manager (Mail Motor Service) BE/B.Tech in Mechanical or Automobile Engineering from recognized University/Institute 3 years' experience in operation of Motor Transport Traffic in a Govt./Semi Govt./PSU/Company listed on Indian stock exchange(s)
Junior Technical Officer (Sugar & Veg Oils) B.Tech/BE (Oil Technology) OR B.Sc. with PG Diploma in Sugar Technology from recognized University/Institute 1 year experience in Sugar Technology or Edible Oils
Junior Technical Officer (Chemical Engineering) - NSI Kanpur Master's Degree in Chemical Engineering from recognized University/Institute 3 years' research/practical/teaching experience in Chemical Engineering from recognized College/University/Autonomous body OR 3 years' experience in Sugar factory/Distillery/Chemical Plant design or operation
Scientific Officer (Mechanical) - NTH Master's Degree in Physics OR Degree in Mechanical Engineering OR Degree in Metallurgy from recognized University/Institute 1 year experience in Mechanical Testing Laboratory OR 1 year experience in Research and Development in allied field
Deputy Superintending Archaeological Engineer (ASI) Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering from a recognized University/Institute 3 years' experience in planning, execution and maintenance of civil engineering works in Government Sector or any recognized Archaeological or heritage site
Associate Professor, Electrical Engineering (CME Pune) BE/B.Tech in Electrical Engineering AND ME/M.Tech in Electrical Engineering (Power System or related specializations) with First Class; AND Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering Minimum 5 years' experience in Teaching/Research/Industry, of which 2 years post-Ph.D. experience is desirable
Associate Professor, Mechanical Engineering (CME Pune) BE/B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering AND ME/M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering (Heat Transfer/Thermal Engineering or related specializations) with First Class; AND Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering Minimum 5 years' experience in Teaching/Research/Industry, of which 2 years post-Ph.D. experience is desirable
Lecturer (Persian Language) - SFL Master's degree in Persian Language from a recognized University/Institution 1 year experience in teaching or translation from Persian to English/Hindi or vice-versa
Lecturer (Tibetan Language) - SFL Master's degree in Tibetan Language from a recognized University/Institution 1 year experience in teaching or translation from Tibetan to English/Hindi or vice-versa
Scientist-B (Atmospheric Sciences) - IMD Master's Degree in Engineering & Technology in Atmospheric Sciences/Ocean Science/Hydrology/Atmospheric Physics/Atmospheric Ocean Science & Technology/Radar & Microwave Engineering from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)
Scientist-B (General Meteorology) - IMD Master's Degree in Science in Mathematics/Physics/Atmospheric Science or Physics/Meteorology from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)
Scientist-B (Instrumentation) - IMD Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology in Electronics & Communication/Mechanical/IT/Instrumentation/Mechatronics/Electrical/Computer Science from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)
Deputy Director (Civil Engineering) - CICEF Bachelor's Degree in Civil Engineering OR Master's Degree in Civil Engineering from recognized University/Institute For B.E./B.Tech holders: 4 years in harbour projects and maritime structures in any Govt. organization. For M.E./M.Tech holders: 2 years in harbour projects and maritime structures
Joint Assistant Director (Police Wireless) B.Tech/BE/B.Sc. (Engnr.) in Electronics/Electronics & Telecom/Electronics & Communication/IT/Computer Science/ICT/Electrical Engineering (with Telecom special subject) OR Master's degree in Electronics/Computer Science/IT/Artificial Intelligence/Physics with Electronics/Communication/Wireless/Radio specialization Nil (Experience is desirable, not essential)
Assistant Executive Engineer - CGWB Bachelor's Degree in Drilling/Mining/Mechanical/Electrical/Civil/Agricultural Engineering or Petroleum Technology from recognized University/Institute 2 years' experience in operation/maintenance/repair of Drilling Rigs or construction of Tube wells or Ground Water Development and Management
Deputy Director of Mines Safety (Mining) Degree in Mining Engineering from recognized University/Institute OR pass in Section A & B of AMIE in Mining Engineering Branch (enrolled up to 31.05.2013 only); AND First Class Mine Manager's Certificate (Unrestricted) under Coal Mines Regulations, 2017 or Metalliferous Mines Regulations, 1961 10 years' experience in mining in large and mechanized underground mines
Assistant Director Grade-I (IEDS) (Chemical) - MSME Master's Degree in Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry OR Bachelor's Degree in Chemical Technology or Chemical Engineering from recognized University/Institute Nil (No experience specified)

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application i.e. 12-06-2026 (1800 Hrs). Mere possession of minimum essential qualifications does not entitle candidates to be called for interview - UPSC may shortlist candidates based on desirable qualifications, higher educational qualifications, higher experience, or by holding a Recruitment Test. All candidates in Government service (permanent/temporary, other than casual/adhoc/daily wages) must submit a declaration that they have informed their Head of Office/Department in writing that they have applied.

Criteria Details
Citizenship Citizen of India, or subject of Nepal/Bhutan, or Tibetan refugee (came before 01-01-1962 intending to permanently settle in India), or person of Indian origin migrated from Pakistan/Burma/Sri Lanka/East African countries intending to permanently settle in India - subject to Government of India eligibility certificate where applicable.
Educational Qualification Varies by post - from Bachelor's degree in Engineering to Master's degree/Ph.D. in the relevant discipline. Refer post-wise details in the notification.
Experience Varies by post - from nil to 10 years of relevant experience. Part-time/daily wages/visiting/guest faculty experience will NOT be counted. Refer post-wise details.
OBC - Non Creamy Layer OBC candidates must submit a declaration that they do not belong to the Creamy Layer. The closing date of receipt of Online Recruitment Application is the crucial date for OBC non-creamy layer status.
PwBD Eligibility PwBD candidates with disability not less than 40% are eligible. Those with "likely to improve" (temporary disability) are NOT eligible for reservation. Candidates can apply for suitable posts even if not reserved for PwBD - but will be considered by general merit standard without relaxation.
Government Employees Persons already in regular government service must submit a declaration that they have informed their Head of Office/Department in writing of their application. Casual/adhoc/daily wages/hourly paid/contract employees should apply directly without such declaration.
Equivalent Qualification Candidates claiming equivalent qualifications must produce order/letter indicating the Authority (with number and date) under which it has been treated as equivalent; otherwise the application will be rejected.

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limits are post-specific and are calculated as on the closing date for submission of online recruitment application i.e. 12-06-2026. The age limits shown in the advertisement are the normal limits for UR/EWS candidates. Standard age relaxations are applicable for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), and PwBD (10 years) for vacancies reserved/suitable for them. OBC/SC/ST/PwBD candidates applying against unreserved posts are not entitled to age relaxation.

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (for vacancies reserved for them)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (for vacancies reserved for them)
PwBD (General) 10 years relaxation (for vacancies reserved/suitable for them)
Ex-Servicemen / Commissioned Officers including ECOs/SSCOs As per prescribed proforma from competent authority - as per Concessions & Relaxations section of UPSC notification
Central / UT Government Employees/Servants As per prescribed proforma from competent authority issued after date of advertisement

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee is to be paid online only and is non-refundable. Fee once paid shall not be refunded under any circumstance nor can it be held in reserve for any other examination or selection. Applications without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected without any representation being entertained.

Category Application Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male candidates) Rs. 25/- (Non-Refundable) - via Visa/Master/Rupay Credit/Debit Card/UPI/Internet Banking
SC / ST / PwBD / Women (all communities) NIL (Exempted from fee payment)

UPSC Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be made by Recruitment by Selection - through Interview or Recruitment Test followed by Interview as decided by UPSC on a post-by-post basis. In the event of a large number of applications, UPSC will adopt shortlisting criteria including desirable qualifications, higher educational qualifications, higher experience, experience before/after essential qualification, or by conducting a Recruitment Test. The summoning of candidates for interview conveys no assurance of selection. Appointment orders will be issued by the respective Ministry.

Stage 1 - Application Scrutiny and Shortlisting

All applications are scrutinized for eligibility. If applications are large in number, UPSC may shortlist based on: (a) Desirable Qualifications; (b) higher educational qualifications; (c) higher relevant experience; (d) counting experience before/after essential qualification; (e) invoking experience even where not mentioned as EQ/DQ; or (f) by holding a Recruitment Test. Candidates should mention all qualifications and experience in the relevant field over and above the minimum qualifications.

Stage 2 - Recruitment Test (RT) followed by Interview (wherever applicable)

If selection is made by Recruitment Test followed by Interview, candidates must achieve minimum suitability at both stages. Minimum interview marks: UR – 50, OBC – 45, SC/ST/PH – 40 (out of 100 total interview marks). Minimum marks for Recruitment Test shall be decided by UPSC on a case-by-case basis.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Selected candidates must be in sound bodily health and must undergo medical examination and satisfy medical authority as required by the Government.

How to Apply for UPSC Recruitment 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/. The UPSC online portal has four modules: Account Creation, Universal Registration (URN), Common Application Form (CAF), and Post/Vacancy-specific module. The first three modules can be filled at any time; the fourth (vacancy-specific) module must be filled during the application window. Candidates who have already registered and have a URN can directly log in and fill the vacancy-specific module. Candidates are strongly advised to use Aadhaar Card as the ID document for seamless verification. Applications once submitted are final - no correction/alteration/modification is permitted after submission. Candidates must take a printout of the finally submitted application and carry it along with original documents if called for interview.

Documents to be Uploaded at the time of Online Application:

  • Live photograph captured via webcam/camera during CAF filling (mandatory)
  • Scanned recent passport-size photograph (as per instructions on UPSC website)
  • Scanned signature (three signatures one below the other on plain white paper, black ink)
  • Matriculation/10th Standard certificate or marksheet as proof of Date of Birth
  • Degree/Diploma certificate as proof of educational qualification; if not available, provisional certificate + all year marksheets
  • Experience Certificate(s) in prescribed format from Head(s) of Organisation(s) - mentioning duration, basic pay, consolidated pay, and nature of duties
  • Caste/Community certificate (SC/ST/OBC) in prescribed proforma from Competent Authority
  • OBC Non-Creamy Layer declaration in prescribed format
  • EWS certificate in prescribed proforma
  • PwBD Disability Certificate from Medical Board constituted by Central/State Government
  • Age relaxation certificate for Ex-Servicemen/Central/UT Government Employees in prescribed proforma
  • Order/letter for equivalent qualification (if applicable) citing Authority with number and date
  • Name change documents (if applicable - marriage certificate/divorce decree/affidavit as per situation)
  • Documentary support for any other claim made (Professional Registration, Publications, GATE/NET, etc.)

Printout of the finally submitted Online Recruitment Application must be retained - candidates will need to bring it to the interview along with original certificates. Candidates are NOT required to send printouts or documents by post or by hand to UPSC.

Apply Online at: https://upsconline.nic.in/ora/

For queries: Helpline No. 011-24041001 or 011-40303444 | Email: [email protected] (10:00 AM to 5:30 PM on all working days during the application window).

Important Instructions for UPSC Advertisement No. 05/2026 Candidates

  • Online applications open from 23-05-2026 and close on 12-06-2026 at 1800 Hrs - candidates must apply well in advance without waiting for the closing date.
  • All candidates must enter correct and active email addresses - all correspondence including interview schedules will be communicated only through the registered email ID.
  • Applications once submitted are final - no correction/alteration/modification in any field is allowed after submission. No withdrawal of applications is permitted.
  • The date for determining eligibility in every respect is the closing date for submission of online recruitment application (12-06-2026).
  • Filling wrong information may lead to rejection through computer-based shortlisting apart from debarment by the Commission.
  • Face authentication will be mandatory for all candidates at the examination venue/Interview Hall - candidates must enter well in time for face authentication and frisking.
  • Mobile phones (even in switched-off mode), pagers, smart watches, pen drives, cameras, Bluetooth devices, or any electronic/communication device are strictly banned at the examination/interview venue. Violation leads to disciplinary action including ban from future examinations/recruitment.
  • Candidates must not bring valuables or bags to the venue - no arrangement for safe-keeping will be made and UPSC will not be responsible for any loss.
  • The Commission will contribute towards interview travelling expenses at Second Class Mail railway fare rate by shortest route - candidates will be informed of details when called for interview.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify a candidate.
  • Summoning for interview conveys no assurance of selection - appointment orders will be issued by the respective Ministry.
  • UPSC may grant higher initial pay to candidates adjudged meritorious in the interview.
  • The Commission does not enter into correspondence with candidates about reasons for non-selection - no interim enquiry about results will be entertained.
  • Prescribed proforma for various certificates are available at https://upsc.gov.in under Recruitment → Forms of Certificates.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest National Small Industries Corporation Limited (NSIC) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Young Professional posts! Published vide Advertisement No. NSIC/HR/YP/01/2026 at the official website of NSIC, this new recruitment drive offers 17 openings across 12 specialised domains for eligible candidates. Applicants below 40 years of age with relevant graduation/post-graduation/professional qualification and minimum 2 years of work experience in the relevant field can apply online from 23-05-2026 to 08-06-2026. Find all details about the NSIC Young Professional new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The National Small Industries Corporation Limited (NSIC), the premier Mini-Ratna Government of India Enterprise under the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, has officially released Advertisement No. NSIC/HR/YP/01/2026 for engagement of 17 Young Professionals on contract basis in the Corporation and/or its subsidiaries. The notification includes complete details on post-wise vacancies, qualification and experience requirements, consolidated pay, tenure, selection process, application procedure, and terms and conditions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official NSIC website.

  • Download NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Overview

NSIC is recruiting 17 Young Professional posts on contract basis across 12 domains with relevant graduation/PG/professional qualification, last date 08 June 2026.

Organization Name National Small Industries Corporation Limited (NSIC), A Government of India Enterprise under Ministry of MSME, New Delhi
Post Name Young Professional (on Contract Basis) — 12 Domain Specialisations
Department Human Resources, NSIC Corporate Office / Subsidiaries
Total Vacancies 17 (across 12 specialised posts)
Consolidated Pay Rs. 40,000/- to Rs. 70,000/- per month (all inclusive). Annual increment not exceeding 5% subject to satisfactory performance and maximum cap of Rs. 70,000/-. No DA, HRA, CGHS, or other allowances.
Tenure Initially 1 year, extendable by 1 year at a time up to a maximum of 5 years, subject to satisfactory annual performance review based on defined KPIs.
Place of Posting Head Office, New Delhi. NSIC reserves the right to post the selected candidate at any of its offices across India.
Application Mode Online only via NSIC Career section: www.nsic.co.in
Selection Mode Shortlisting based on application + Interaction / Interview by Selection Committee
Official Website https://www.nsic.co.in

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement May 2026 (Advt. No. NSIC/HR/YP/01/2026)
Opening of Online Application 23-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application 08-06-2026
Eligibility / Qualification Cut-off Date 08-06-2026 (qualifications and experience must be acquired on or before this date)
Age Calculated As On Closing date of advertisement (08-06-2026)
Interview / Interaction Date To be notified to shortlisted candidates by email
Joining Date (after offer) Within 15 days of offer of engagement

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 17 Young Professional posts have been announced across 12 domain specialisations on contract basis. NSIC reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of vacancies in any category at its sole discretion without assigning any reason. Candidates may apply for only one post. If a candidate submits more than one application, only the application submitted first (as per system-generated date and time) will be considered and the remaining will be rejected.

Sr. No. Post Name No. of Posts
1 Young Professional – Software & Web Development 02
2 Young Professional – Digital Media & Social Media Management 02
3 Young Professional – Market & Economic Analysis 02
4 Young Professional – Digital Marketing, E-Commerce etc. 01
5 Young Professional – Company Secretary 01
6 Young Professional – Material Handling 01
7 Young Professional – Market Research, Data Interpretation & Financial Analysis 01
8 Young Professional – Civil Engineer 01
9 Young Professional – Electrical Engineer 01
10 Young Professional – MSME Business Outreach 02
11 Young Professional – Venture Equity Fund Senior Analyst 02
12 Young Professional – IT Specialist for Venture Capital Fund 01
Total 17

Note: For Post No. 11 (Venture Equity Fund Senior Analyst), the Competent Authority may offer a higher pay slab at its sole discretion based on qualification and experience.

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions (qualification and experience) as on 08-06-2026. Post-wise qualification and experience requirements are detailed below. Only candidates with the relevant domain qualification and minimum 2 years of work experience in the relevant field are eligible to apply.

Post Name Educational Qualification Experience & Key Competencies
Young Professional – Software & Web Development B.E. / B.Tech in Computer Science or combination Engineering OR MCA from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Web Development (.NET), REST-API, Website hosting, MS SQL Development, Windows Server Management
Young Professional – Digital Media & Social Media Management Bachelor's degree in Mass Communication / Digital Media / Journalism / Public Relations. MBA is an added advantage. Minimum 2 years. Social media management, content creation, digital marketing, analytics tools, Photoshop/Illustrator/Canva proficiency. Prior experience with govt./PSU bodies is an added advantage.
Young Professional – Market & Economic Analysis Graduate degree with MBA (Business Analytics / Data Analytics / Strategy / Finance / Operations / Marketing) OR MA/M.Sc. in Economics / Econometrics / Physical Science / Humanities from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Environmental scanning, MIS report preparation, MS Excel (Pivot Tables, Macros desirable), documentation as per PSU/government standards
Young Professional – Digital Marketing, E-Commerce etc. Bachelor's degree in New Media Design / Animation / Mass Communication / Digital Media or related field. MBA is an added advantage. Minimum 2 years. Graphic design for digital platforms — Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, Canva; social media creatives, infographics, banners, brand identity management
Young Professional – Company Secretary Degree in Commerce + Qualified Company Secretary (CS). MBA preferred. Minimum 2 years. ROC filings, statutory records maintenance, Board Meetings / AGM compliance, Companies Act 2013 compliance
Young Professional – Material Handling MBA or equivalent with specialization in Finance / Marketing from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Sales and marketing of raw materials (Aluminium, Copper, Iron & Steel) or finished goods. Procurement support, supply chain coordination, MSME/CPSE framework knowledge, MS Office proficiency
Young Professional – Market Research, Data Interpretation & Financial Analysis MBA or equivalent in Finance / Marketing from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Market research, data interpretation, financial analysis, GeM portal operations, e-Tendering experience, MSME ecosystem knowledge
Young Professional – Civil Engineer B.E. / B.Tech in Civil Engineering from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Construction/maintenance of buildings/STPs/WTPs, tender documents, contract management, building bye-laws, STAAD PRO / ETABS / AUTOCAD / REVIT / BIM knowledge preferred
Young Professional – Electrical Engineer B.E. / B.Tech in Electrical or combination Engineering (Electrical & Electronics etc.) from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Electrical sub-stations, Solar Panel installations, Green Buildings, STPs/WTPs; tender documents, contract management; STAAD PRO / ETABS / AUTOCAD / BIM knowledge preferred
Young Professional – MSME Business Outreach MBA or equivalent in Finance / Marketing from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. NBFC/Bank experience in MSME relationship management. Knowledge of banking/loan products is mandatory. Lead generation, client acquisition, revenue driving, cross-functional team leadership
Young Professional – Venture Equity Fund Senior Analyst Chartered Accountant (CA) OR MBA (Finance) or equivalent from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Fund accounting, treasury operations, private equity fund administration, capital calls/drawdowns, SEBI AIF regulations, RBI guidelines, Income Tax compliance, Excel and financial data management
Young Professional – IT Specialist for Venture Capital Fund B.E. / B.Tech in Computer Science or combination Engineering OR MCA from a recognized University/Institute Minimum 2 years. Full stack development — C++ .NET, MS SQL, ASP.NET, HTML, CSS, Bootstrap; web portal management, SEBI reporting compliance, data integrity and security

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Upper age limit is 40 years as on the closing date of advertisement (08-06-2026). No minimum age is specified beyond the implied requirement of 2 years of relevant post-qualification work experience. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates is as per Government of India guidelines, where applicable.

Category Upper Age Limit
General / Unreserved 40 years (as on 08-06-2026)
SC / ST Age relaxation as per GoI rules (check official notification)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Age relaxation as per GoI rules (check official notification)
PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) Age relaxation as per GoI rules (check official notification)

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

The official notification does not mention any application fee for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026. Candidates should verify on the NSIC Career portal (www.nsic.co.in) at the time of applying, as any applicable fee details will be specified on the online application portal.

Category Fees
All Categories No application fee mentioned in notification. Verify on official portal at time of applying.

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection is through shortlisting of applications followed by an interaction / interview by the NSIC Selection Committee. Mere submission of application or fulfillment of eligibility criteria does not entitle a candidate to be called for interview. Call letters and other information will be sent to shortlisted candidates by email only and may also be published on the NSIC corporate website. NSIC reserves the right to upgrade eligibility criteria if a large number of applications are received.

Stage 1 - Application Shortlisting

Applications received within the deadline will be screened for eligibility as per the advertisement. Candidates who meet the prescribed qualification, experience, and age criteria will be shortlisted based on the relevance and quality of their profile. Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. Any request to add additional information after submission will not be entertained.

Stage 2 - Interaction / Interview

Shortlisted candidates will be called for an interaction / interview before the NSIC Selection Committee. Date, time, and venue of the interaction will be communicated by email only to the registered email ID. Candidates must keep their email ID active for at least 3 months from the date of application. NSIC will not be responsible for non-delivery or late delivery of email due to any reason.

Stage 3 - Medical Clearance & Police Verification

Selected candidates must submit a Statement of Good Health from a recognized Physician prior to commencement of work. Antecedent/police verification of selected candidates will be done as per latest instructions issued by the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA). If police verification is received as negative, the contract will cease to exist with immediate effect without any notice.

NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 - Post-wise Work Location & Key Details

All Young Professionals will be primarily posted at NSIC Head Office, New Delhi. However, NSIC reserves the right to post them at any of its offices across India. Working hours are 9:30 AM to 6:00 PM. The engagement is on a full-time basis and the incumbent cannot take up any other assignment during the engagement period.

Detail Condition
Primary Posting Location Head Office, New Delhi (may be posted anywhere in India at NSIC's discretion)
Working Hours 9:30 AM to 6:00 PM
Leave Entitlement 12 days per year on pro rata basis
Tenure Initially 1 year; extendable by 1 year at a time up to maximum 5 years (subject to KPI-based annual performance review)
Annual Increment Not exceeding 5% of monthly remuneration subject to satisfactory performance and maximum cap of Rs. 70,000/-
Notice Period (Termination / Resignation) 30 days notice (by either party) or compensation in lieu thereof
Joining Period Within 15 days of offer of engagement; failing which offer stands withdrawn automatically

How to Apply for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online through the CAREER section of www.nsic.co.in only. No other mode of application will be accepted. The online application link is active from 23-05-2026. The application process is in 3 stages. Candidates should keep all documents ready before starting the application as incomplete applications cannot be modified after submission.

Stage 1 — Registration: Fill in Name, Mobile Number, Email ID, and Date of Birth. Verify email via the verification link sent to the registered email ID. Login and fill in basic details.

Stage 2 — Document Upload (Self-attested copies):

  • Aadhaar Card
  • PAN Card
  • Caste / PwBD Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size colour photograph with white background (JPG/JPEG/PNG, recent, under 100 KB)
  • Signature (JPG/JPEG/PNG, under 100 KB)
  • Class X Certificate / School Leaving Certificate — as Date of Birth proof
  • Mark-sheets of each year / consolidated mark-sheet of Graduation / Post Graduation
  • Graduation / Post Graduation Degree Certificate
  • Experience Certificates (Service Certificate showing start and end of employment OR Appointment Letter + Resignation/Relieving Letter OR salary slips duly stamped and signed)
  • Salary slips / salary certificates as proof of current compensation
  • Detailed CV
  • CGPA to percentage conversion proof from University/Institute (if applicable)
  • Gazette notification / affidavit for name/surname change (if applicable)
  • Company name change proof / inter-group transfer proof (if applicable)

Stage 3 — Submission: After completing all stages, an Application Number will be generated and sent to the registered mobile number via SMS and email. This Application Number must be preserved for all future references.

Documents to be Produced at Interview / Interaction:

  • Printout of submitted online application
  • All original documents (listed above) along with self-attested photocopies
  • Class X Certificate / School Leaving Certificate (original) for Date of Birth
  • All original mark-sheets and degree certificates (Graduation / Post Graduation)
  • Original experience / service certificates and salary slips
  • Original Aadhaar Card, PAN Card
  • Original caste certificate / PwBD certificate (if applicable)
  • Statement of Good Health from a recognized Physician (for joining, if selected)

Apply Online at: www.nsic.co.in → Career Section → NSIC/HR/YP/01/2026 → Apply Online

For queries: Visit www.nsic.co.in | All corrigenda/updates regarding engagement process will be notified on NSIC's website only.

Important Instructions for NSIC Young Professional Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT A REGULAR EMPLOYMENT. Engagement of Young Professionals is purely on a contractual basis for consultancy purposes. Young Professionals are not entitled to any benefit or compensation available to regular NSIC employees, including DA, HRA, CGHS, Medical reimbursement, or Provident Fund.
  • The engagement is on a full-time basis. The selected candidate cannot take up any other assignment in any other company during the period of engagement.
  • Candidates may apply for only one post. If more than one application is submitted, only the first application (by system-generated date and time) will be considered and the remaining will be summarily rejected.
  • Prescribed qualifications and experience must have been acquired on or before 08-06-2026. Qualifications or experience acquired after the cut-off will not be considered.
  • Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected. Once submitted, no request to add or modify information will be entertained under any circumstances.
  • NSIC reserves the right to upgrade eligibility criteria and increase or decrease the number of vacancies in any category without assigning any reason.
  • Call letters and all future communications will be sent by email only. Candidates must ensure their registered email ID is active for at least 3 months. NSIC bears no responsibility for non-delivery of emails.
  • Shortlisting for interview is at NSIC's sole discretion — fulfilling eligibility criteria does not guarantee a call for interview.
  • The selected candidate must join within 15 days of the offer of engagement. Failure to join within this period will result in automatic withdrawal of the offer.
  • Income Tax and other applicable taxes will be deducted at source. NSIC will issue TDS certificates. GST, as applicable, shall be admissible to Young Professionals. The Young Professional is responsible for paying their own Income Tax as applicable.
  • NSIC or the Competent Authority may terminate the contract at any time by giving 30 days' notice or paying 30 days' compensation in lieu thereof, without assigning any reason.
  • Antecedent/police verification of selected candidates will be conducted as per MHA guidelines. A negative police verification report will result in immediate termination of the contract without any notice.
  • No abbreviations should be used in the application where full forms are indicated — applications with abbreviations in such fields are liable for rejection.
  • NSIC reserves the right to cancel, modify, withdraw, or postpone this recruitment notice at any time without assigning any reason.
  • All corrigenda and updates will be published on www.nsic.co.in only — candidates should check the website regularly.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Indian Navy 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry Scheme Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Permanent Commissioned Officer posts in Technical Branches! Published on 29-05-2026 at the official Indian Navy recruitment website, this new recruitment drive offers 60 vacancies (30 each for Mechanical Engineering and Electronics & Communication Engineering) for eligible unmarried men and women candidates. Applicants who have appeared for JEE (Main) 2026 and passed 10+2 with at least 70% aggregate in PCM and 50% in English can apply online from 29-05-2026 to 18-06-2026. Find all details about the Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme January 2027 course, including eligibility, SSB selection process, and online application here.

FOLLOW US

Indian Navy 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 Notification PDF

The Indian Navy has officially invited applications from unmarried men and women candidates for becoming Permanent Commissioned Officers in Technical Branches under the 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme. Selected candidates will undergo a four-year B.Tech course at the prestigious Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, JEE (Main) 2026 requirement, SSB selection procedure, medical standards, training details, and online application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification and instructions on the official website before applying.

  • Download Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme January 2027 Official Notification PDF

Indian Navy 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry Scheme January 2027 Overview

Indian Navy is recruiting 60 Permanent Commissioned Officer vacancies in Technical Branches through the 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme for the course commencing January 2027, last date for online application 18 June 2026.

Organization Name Indian Navy (Naval Headquarters)
Advertisement No. CBC / / /26-27
Scheme Name 10+2 (B.Tech) Cadet Entry Scheme — Permanent Commission
Course Commencing January 2027
Training Institute Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala
Total Vacancies 60 (30 – Mechanical Engineering + 30 – Electronics & Communication Engineering; maximum 08 vacancies for women — 04 each for Mechanical Engg and Electronics & Communication Engg)
Type of Commission Permanent Commission (Technical Branch)
Degree Awarded B.Tech (awarded by Jawaharlal Nehru University — JNU) on completion of 4-year course
Training Cost Entire cost of training including books, reading material, entitled clothing and messing borne by the Indian Navy
Application Mode Online only — at www.joinindiannavy.gov.in
Selection Mode JEE (Main) 2026 CRL-based shortlisting → SSB Interview → Medical Examination → Police & Character Verification → Merit List
Date of Opening of Application 29-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application 18-06-2026
Official Website https://www.joinindiannavy.gov.in

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Opening of Online Application 29-05-2026
Last Date for Online Application 18-06-2026
Age Calculated As On Date of birth between 02-07-2007 and 01-01-2010 (both dates inclusive)
SSB Interview Schedule September 2026 onwards (at Bengaluru / Bhopal / Kolkata / Visakhapatnam)
Course Commencement January 2027 at Indian Naval Academy, Ezhimala

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 60 vacancies have been announced for Permanent Commission in Technical Branches of the Indian Navy. Candidates must give their preference of Mechanical Engineering or Electronics & Communication Engineering during online application. Based on merit and qualification during training at INA, 07 candidates from Mechanical Engineering will be streamed to B.Tech in Naval Architecture & Shipbuilding at NCW, Kochi. Vacancies are tentative and may be changed depending on service requirements. Multiple applications by any candidate will lead to cancellation of candidature.

Technical Branch Total Vacancies Gender Age
Mechanical Engineering** 30 Men and Women (Maximum 04 vacancies for Women) Born between 02-07-2007 and 01-01-2010 (both dates inclusive)
Electronics & Communication Engineering 30 Men and Women (Maximum 04 vacancies for Women)
Total 60 Maximum 08 vacancies for Women overall  

** Based on merit and qualification during training at INA, 07 candidates from Mechanical Engineering will be streamed to B.Tech in Naval Architecture & Shipbuilding at NCW, Kochi.

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of application. Only unmarried men and women fulfilling the conditions of nationality as laid down by the Government of India are eligible. Any candidate found to be married or who marries while under training will be discharged from service and shall be liable to refund full training cost including pay, allowances and any other expenditure incurred by the Government.

Criteria Details
Nationality As per conditions of nationality laid down by the Government of India.
Marital Status Unmarried men and women only. Candidates who marry while under training will be discharged and must refund all training costs.
Educational Qualification Passed Senior Secondary Examination (10+2 pattern) or equivalent from any recognized Board with at least 70% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics (PCM) and at least 50% marks in English (either in Class X or Class XII).
JEE (Main) 2026 Requirement Must have appeared for JEE (Main) 2026 exam (for B.E./B.Tech). Call up for SSB will be issued on the basis of JEE (Main) All India Common Rank List (CRL) 2026 published by NTA. Candidates must fill their CRL Rank in the application.
Medical Standards All candidates recommended by SSB must undergo medical examination applicable to 10+2 (B.Tech) entry. Candidates already declared medically fit for NDA/INA Jan 27 need not repeat medical examination. No relaxation in medical standards on any grounds. Change of medical hospital/centre is not permissible under any circumstances.
Narcotics Use/possession of narcotics is banned. Candidates may be tested during SSB/medicals/training/service. If found using/possessing narcotics at any stage, candidate will be debarred from joining or removed from service.

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must have been born between 02 July 2007 and 01 January 2010 (both dates inclusive) to be eligible for the January 2027 course. There is no separate age relaxation mentioned — the age band is fixed for all candidates.

Category Age Band
All candidates (Men and Women) Born between 02-07-2007 and 01-01-2010 (both dates inclusive)

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 - Application Fee

The notification does not mention any application fee. Candidates are advised to check the official Indian Navy recruitment website www.joinindiannavy.gov.in for any fee-related information before submitting the online application.

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on JEE (Main) 2026 CRL-based shortlisting, followed by SSB Interview, Medical Examination, and Police & Character Verification. Merit list will be prepared based on SSB marks and medical fitness. Candidates will be appointed subject to Police Verification, Character Verification, and availability of vacancies in the entry. Mere inclusion in the common merit list does not confer a vested right to appointment. Mere empanelment under no circumstances can be treated as final selection.

Stage 1 - Shortlisting Based on JEE (Main) 2026 CRL

Naval Headquarters reserves the right to fix the cut-off for shortlisting of applications for SSB based on JEE (Main) All India Common Rank List (CRL) 2026. All candidates must fill their CRL Rank in the application. Shortlisted candidates will be informed about their SSB interview schedule through email and SMS. Candidates must not change their email/mobile number until the selection process is over.

Stage 2 - Service Selection Board (SSB) Interview

SSB interviews for shortlisted candidates will be scheduled at Bengaluru / Bhopal / Kolkata / Visakhapatnam from September 2026 onwards. Change of SSB Centre is not permissible under any circumstances. Candidates must download the call-up letter on receiving intimation from Naval Headquarters via SMS/email. AC 3-tier rail fare is admissible for SSB interview if appearing for the first time for this particular type of commission — candidates must bring a photocopy of the first page of passbook or cheque leaf with Name, A/C No., and IFSC details. No compensation is admissible in case of any injury sustained during SSB tests.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

All candidates recommended by SSB must undergo medical examination as per standards applicable to 10+2 (B.Tech) entry. No relaxation in medical standards on any grounds. Candidates already declared medically fit for NDA/INA Jan 27 need not repeat medical examination. Change of medical hospital/centre is not permissible under any circumstances.

Stage 4 - Common Merit List & Appointment

Final merit list will be prepared based on SSB marks and medical fitness. Candidates will be appointed subject to Police Verification, Character Verification, and availability of vacancies. All vacancies are to be filled as per service requirement and statutory guidelines issued from time to time.

Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme 2026 - Training Details

Selected candidates will be inducted as Cadets for the four-year B.Tech Course at the Indian Naval Academy (INA), Ezhimala, in Electronics & Communication Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Naval Architecture & Shipbuilding (only for 07 selected candidates from Mechanical Engineering at NCW, Kochi) as per Naval requirements. On completion of the course, B.Tech Degree will be awarded by Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU). The entire cost of training including books and reading material will be borne by the Indian Navy. Cadets will also be provided with entitled clothing and messing. Details of pay, allowances, group insurance, gratuity, leave entitlements, and duties of officers are available on www.joinindiannavy.gov.in.

How to Apply for Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme January 2027

Candidates must register and submit their application online only at the official Indian Navy recruitment website www.joinindiannavy.gov.in. No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates are advised to read all instructions on the website carefully before submitting the online application. The application once submitted is final and no request for amendments or changes will be entertained.

Documents to be Scanned and Uploaded (in original JPG/TIFF format):

  • Date of Birth proof (as per 10th/12th Class certificate)
  • 10th Class marksheet
  • 12th Class marksheet
  • JEE (Main) 2026 Score Card indicating Common Rank List (CRL)
  • Recent passport-size colour photograph

Documents to be Produced / Carried at the time of SSB Interview:

  • Print-out of the submitted online application form
  • Original certificates/documents as submitted during online application
  • Photocopy of first page of bank passbook or cheque leaf (showing Name, A/C No., and IFSC details) — for rail fare reimbursement claim

Candidates must ensure that all details including email address and mobile number are mandatory fields and are correctly filled. Personal particulars must match exactly with Matriculation/12th Class certificates. Candidature will be cancelled if any declaration is found wrong at any stage of selection.

Apply Online at: https://www.joinindiannavy.gov.in/en/account/account/state

For complete details on SSB procedure, pay & allowances, medical standards, and other information: Visit https://www.joinindiannavy.gov.in

Important Instructions for Indian Navy 10+2 B.Tech Cadet Entry Scheme January 2027 Candidates

  • Only unmarried men and women fulfilling conditions of nationality as laid down by the Government of India are eligible to apply.
  • Multiple applications by any candidate for this course will lead to cancellation of candidature.
  • Vacancies indicated are tentative and may change depending on service requirements.
  • Candidates must not change their email/mobile number provided in the application till the selection process is over — all SSB call-up intimations are sent on these details.
  • Change of SSB Centre for examination/interview is not permissible under any circumstances.
  • Change of medical hospital/centre is not permissible under any circumstances. There is no relaxation in medical standards on any grounds.
  • Candidates who have already been declared medically fit for NDA/INA Jan 27 need not undergo repeat medical examination.
  • No compensation is admissible in case of any injury sustained as a result of tests during SSB interviews.
  • AC 3-tier rail fare is admissible for SSB interview only if appearing for the first time for this particular type of commission — carry photocopy of passbook/cheque leaf with Name, A/C No., and IFSC details.
  • Any candidate who is found to be married or marries while under training will be discharged from service and shall be liable to refund full training cost including pay & allowances and any other expenditure incurred by the Government.
  • Use/possession of narcotics is banned — candidates may be tested at any stage of selection/medicals/training/service.
  • Mere inclusion in the common merit list does not confer a vested right to appointment. Mere empanelment is not an enforceable claim to the post.
  • The application is subject to subsequent scrutiny and can be rejected if found ineligible/invalid at any point of time.
  • Candidature will be cancelled if any declaration is found wrong at any stage of selection.
  • If any scanned document is not legible/readable, the application will be rejected.
  • The application once submitted is final — no request for amendments/changes will be entertained.

AI Engineering Services Limited (AIESL) has announced an Internal Staff Employment Notice for the post of Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (AME)! Published on 21-05-2026 vide Ref. No. AIESL/HR-HQ/2026/272, this internal recruitment drive offers 65 tentative vacancies exclusively for serving employees of AIESL. Eligible candidates with passed DGCA B1/B2 Modules or DGCA License and minimum 5 years of aviation experience (including at least 2 years in AIESL) can apply online via Google Form on or before 26-05-2026 (5:00 PM). Find all details about AIESL Trainee AME Internal Recruitment 2026, including eligibility, vacancy details, bond conditions, and application process here.

FOLLOW US

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

AI Engineering Services Limited (A wholly owned subsidiary of AI Assets Holding Limited), CRA Building, 2nd Floor, Safdarjung Airport, New Delhi - 110003 has officially released an Internal Staff Employment Notice dated 21st May 2026 (Ref. No. AIESL/HR-HQ/2026/272) for filling up the post of Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer and to form a panel of suitable candidates for future requirement. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, qualification, age limit, reservation, selection procedure, training period, bond conditions, placement, career progression, and structure of AME trainee induction. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official AIESL website.

  • Download AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 Overview

AIESL is offering 65 Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineers (tentative) through an internal notice exclusively for serving AIESL employees, last date 26 May 2026 (5:00 PM).

Organization Name AI Engineering Services Limited - AIESL (A wholly owned subsidiary of AI Assets Holding Limited), New Delhi
Post Name Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (Trainee AME)
Department HR - HQ, AI Engineering Services Limited
Total Vacancies 65 (Tentative; may vary depending upon Company requirements)
Pay During Training Existing pay of the selected candidate shall be protected during the period of training; salary protection given if trainee is already drawing a higher salary
Pay After Training Placed in applicable scale of pay as per AIESL Board approved policy on acquisition of DGCA License or equivalent qualification and completion of training
Duration of Training Already a certifying staff (with B1/B2 authorization): 1 month; Already holding Aircraft License within AIESL capability: on getting relevant authorization or 12 months, whichever earlier; Others: 12 months
Application Mode Online only - via Google Form link available on AIESL website (https://forms.gle/P3KpXSsJGJ4dYNh59)
Selection Mode Assessment of eligibility criteria by a duly constituted committee
Mandatory Requirement THIS IS AN INTERNAL NOTICE - Only serving employees of AIESL are eligible to apply. Minimum 2 years of service in AIESL is mandatory.

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Notification 21/05/2026

Last Date of Receipt of Online Application 26-05-2026 (1700 hours / 5:00 PM)

Tentative Date of Selection / Interview To be notified by AIESL after closing date

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 65 tentative vacancies have been announced for the post of Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer, exclusively for serving employees of AIESL. Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen is applicable as per Government of India guidelines. The number of vacancies may vary depending upon Company requirements. A panel of suitable candidates will also be formed for future requirements.

Post Name Total Vacancies Reservation
Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (B1 / B2) 65 (Tentative) As per Govt. guidelines (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-SM)

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

This is an internal employment notice. Only serving employees of AIESL who meet all the prescribed eligibility conditions as on 01-05-2026 are eligible to apply. Candidates who had applied against previous notifications for Trainee - AME are required to apply afresh through the prescribed online application form.

Criteria Details
Eligibility (Mandatory) Only serving employees of AIESL are eligible. External / open market candidates are NOT eligible to apply.
DGCA Qualification (Mandatory) Must have passed all B1 or B2 Modules of DGCA knowledge examinations; OR hold a B1 DGCA License or B2 DGCA License within AIESL capability. RT License would be preferred.
Minimum Aviation Experience Minimum 5 years of current practical maintenance experience on operating aircraft as prescribed under CAR 66, out of which at least 2 years must be in the services of AIESL.
Parent Vertical Candidates will remain in their parent vertical (AIESL / AIX / AASL) and continue with the same type of employment arrangement (Permanent / Fixed Term) after acquiring requisite qualification and absorption in the respective cadre.
Fresh Application Requirement Candidates who had applied against any previous AIESL notification for Trainee AME must apply afresh - prior applications will not be considered.

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Upper age limit is 52 years as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Ex-Servicemen categories.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS Up to 52 years (as on 01-05-2026)
SC / ST / OBC / EWS / Ex-Servicemen As per Government of India guidelines

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

The notification does not mention any application fee for this internal recruitment. Candidates should refer to the official notification / Google Form on the AIESL website for any updates before applying.

AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on assessment of eligibility criteria by a committee constituted for the purpose. No separate written examination has been announced. Final induction into the Trainee AME role is subject to successful completion of training, acquisition of DGCA License, and fulfilment of bond conditions.

Stage 1 - Eligibility Assessment by Committee

Candidates found suitable after assessment of the prescribed eligibility criteria by the duly constituted committee shall be considered for the post. Selected candidates will be designated as Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer till such time they acquire the DGCA license or equivalent qualification within AIESL capability.

Stage 2 - Training and Bond Execution

Selected candidates will be imparted DGCA approved Type Rated B1/B2 training as per Company requirement. Candidates must execute a training bond as per the training policy before commencement of type training. On successful completion of approved training and after fulfilling the experience requirement, candidates must obtain the DGCA License or equivalent qualification. If a candidate fails to acquire the qualification within 4 years of joining as Trainee AME and being offered the type course, they will be reverted to their previous position and the bond amount will be recovered.

Stage 3 - Absorption and Career Progression

On acquiring the DGCA License in the appropriate category and on completion of the training period, the candidate will be placed in the applicable scale of pay. Allocation to Group A or Group B will be done only after all mandatory trainings are completed, depending on individual performance during training and existing vacancies in the respective group. Career progression will follow applicable AIESL policy as per the respective cadre (Permanent/FTE/AIXL/AASL).

Who should NOT apply for AIESL Trainee AME Internal Recruitment 2026?

  • External candidates from the open market - this is strictly an internal employment notice and only serving employees of AIESL are eligible to apply.
  • Serving employees who do not have at least 2 years of service specifically within AIESL - total aviation experience of 5 years is required, with minimum 2 years mandatorily in AIESL.
  • Candidates above 52 years of age as on 01-05-2026, except those eligible for age relaxation as per Government of India guidelines.
  • Candidates who have not passed all B1 or B2 Modules of DGCA knowledge examinations and do not hold a B1 or B2 DGCA License within AIESL capability - DGCA qualification is a hard eligibility condition.
  • Candidates who are unwilling to execute the mandatory indemnity bond of Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakhs) and serve AIESL for a minimum of 5 years from the date of joining or till the age of superannuation, whichever is earlier - no pro-rata waiver or partial relaxation is permitted.
  • Candidates unwilling to execute a separate Type Training Bond prior to commencement of Type Training as per Company training policy.
  • Candidates not willing to be posted to any AIESL station / region as per Company requirement during training and after absorption.
  • Candidates who had applied against previous AIESL notifications for Trainee - AME but have not applied afresh - prior applications will not be considered and fresh application is mandatory.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying AIESL Trainee AME Internal Recruitment 2026?

  • Not applying afresh - candidates who had applied against any previous AIESL notification for Trainee AME must submit a new application through the prescribed Google Form; old applications will not be carried forward.
  • Missing the last date deadline - the application Google Form closes at 1700 hours (5:00 PM) sharp on 26-05-2026; applications received late will be rejected without any reference, irrespective of the reason.
  • Not verifying that all eligibility conditions are met as on 01-05-2026 - the cutoff date for all eligibility is 01-05-2026, not the date of application; candidates must confirm age, experience, and qualification against this date.
  • Not ensuring the minimum 2 years of AIESL service is complete as on the cutoff date - total aviation experience and AIESL-specific experience are two separate conditions, both of which must be independently satisfied.
  • Not accessing the Google Form through the AIESL official website - the form link must be accessed from the official AIESL website to ensure it is the correct and current form.
  • Incomplete filling of the Google Form - since the application is through an online form, all mandatory fields must be filled accurately; incomplete submissions may be rejected.
  • Ignoring the training bond obligation - candidates must be fully aware that the Rs. 5,00,000/- bond is strictly enforceable for the entire 5-year period with no proportional deduction or partial waiver for early exit.
  • Assuming posting location flexibility - selected candidates may be posted to any AIESL station/region as per Company requirement during training and subsequently; candidates must be prepared for inter-station postings.

How to Apply for AIESL Trainee Aircraft Maintenance Engineer Recruitment 2026

Only serving employees of AIESL who meet the prescribed qualifications as of 01-05-2026 may apply. Applications must be submitted online through the Google Form link available on the AIESL website. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates who had applied against any previous notification for Trainee AME must apply afresh. Applications received after the last date (1700 hours, 26-05-2026) will be rejected without any reference.

Apply Online (Google Form) at: https://forms.gle/P3KpXSsJGJ4dYNh59 (also accessible via AIESL website)

Management reserves the right to add, delete, or alter any of the criteria mentioned above and notify accordingly if necessitated.

Important Instructions for AIESL Trainee AME Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS AN INTERNAL STAFF EMPLOYMENT NOTICE. Only serving employees of AIESL are eligible. External/open market candidates must not apply.
  • Selected candidates will be designated as Trainee - Aircraft Maintenance Engineer till such time they acquire the DGCA License or equivalent qualification. They will continue to perform existing duties/functions and any other duties assigned in their present cadre during the training period.
  • The existing pay of selected candidates will be protected during the period of training. If a trainee is already drawing a higher salary, salary protection will be provided as per AIESL Board approved policy.
  • Selected candidates must execute a mandatory indemnity bond of Rs. 5,00,000/- (Rupees Five Lakhs) for serving AIESL for a minimum of 5 years from the date of joining or till superannuation/retirement, whichever is earlier. The bond amount is treated as liquidated damages in the event of premature resignation/separation and no pro-rata relaxation, partial waiver, or proportional deduction for early exit shall be permitted.
  • In addition to the service bond, upon nomination for Type Training, trainees must execute a separate Type Training Bond prior to commencement of training as per Company training policy.
  • If a candidate fails to acquire the DGCA qualification within 4 years of joining as Trainee AME and being offered the type course, they will be reverted to their previous position and the bond amount will be recovered.
  • Allocation to Group A or Group B will be done only after all mandatory trainings are completed, based on individual performance during training and existing vacancies in the respective group.
  • The employment status (Permanent/FTE) and parent vertical (AIESL/AIX/AASL) of the candidate will remain unchanged after acquisition of requisite qualification and absorption into the respective cadre. AASL candidates will remain in AASL; AIXL candidates will remain in AIXL, etc.
  • Candidates can be posted to any AIESL station/region during training and subsequently at any base depending on Company requirement. No preference of posting location is guaranteed.
  • Management reserves the right to add, delete, or alter any of the criteria mentioned in the notice and notify accordingly as necessitated.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 825+ active job listings.

Application Fee NIL for most states; ₹100–500 for some state Supervisor posts
Job Location Within the district / ward of residence
Notification Year 2026

Eligibility Criteria for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

Criteria Anganwadi Worker Anganwadi Helper Supervisor / CDPO
Education 10th / 12th Pass 8th / 10th Pass Graduation (any stream)
Age Limit 18 – 35 years 18 – 35 years 18 – 40 years
Age Relaxation (SC/ST) 5 years 5 years 5 years
Age Relaxation (OBC) 3 years 3 years 3 years
Residency Condition Same ward / village required Same ward / village required Same district (varies)
Marital Status Married women preferred (some states) No restriction No restriction
Gender Female only Female only Female / Both (state-specific)
Application Fee NIL (most states) NIL (most states) ₹100–500 (varies)

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

State Anganwadi Worker Anganwadi Helper Supervisor
Central Govt (Base) ₹4,500/mo ₹2,250/mo -
Punjab ₹11,500/mo ₹7,000/mo ₹25,000/mo
Uttar Pradesh ₹10,000/mo ₹5,000/mo ₹20,000/mo
Maharashtra ₹12,000/mo ₹6,500/mo ₹28,000/mo
Karnataka ₹14,000/mo ₹7,000/mo ₹30,000/mo
Tamil Nadu ₹13,500/mo ₹6,500/mo ₹28,500/mo

Rajasthan ₹10,000/mo ₹5,500/mo ₹22,000/mo
Odisha ₹9,500/mo ₹5,000/mo ₹20,000/mo

Note: Honorarium figures are approximate and may vary. Refer to official state notification for exact figures. Additional benefits include ESI, provident fund (where applicable), and national health insurance.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Documents Required for Anganwadi Application

  • Aadhaar Card (Mandatory)
  • 10th Marksheet & Certificate
  • 12th Marksheet (for AWW)
  • Graduation Certificate (Supervisor)
  • Domicile / Residence Certificate
  • Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC)
  • Passport-size Photograph
  • Signature (scanned)
  • EWS Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.
